Coarse clean sand and gravel on the lower shore with Arenicola. One 0.1 m2 core was taken (entered as a box quadrat). NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

location_name: NE of Royal Fowey Yacht Club (Fowey)
sample_comment: Sediment shore Lower Midshore 2m ACD. Coarse sand gravel (1 to 2mm). Clean well sorted mobile high energy environment. No visible macrofaunal content. (0.1m2/0.01m2 sediment cores taken). This habitat represents the 0.1m2 data. Abundant Scolelepis squamata, Ophelia rathkei and oligochaetes were found in the core sample(entered as box quadrat). NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Sediment coarse sand mixed with fine (poorly sorted) stones and pebbles. No visible macrofaunal community presence noted within sediment sampled. 0.1 m2 core revealed sparse infauna (entered as a box quadrat). NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Sediment was a silty grey mud with a smooth surface and diatom film (brown) covering. No anoxic layer was seen down to the 10cm depth sampled. Visible macrofauna consisted of Hediste diversicolor and small worms probably oligocheates. There were large quantities of organic detritus in the surficial layers. Two cores of sediment taken on the lower shore 1m ACD;- 0.1m2/1mm mesh, and 0.01m2 over 0.5mm mesh. This habitat represents the 0.1m2 data. Abundant Nephtys hombergii were present in the infauna (entered as box quadrat). NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Sediment was a silty grey mud with a smooth surface and diatom film (brown) covering. No anoxic layer was seen down to the 10cm depth sampled. Visible macrofauna consisted of Hediste diversicolor and small worms probably oligocheates. There were large quantities of organic detritus in the surficial layers. Two cores of sediment taken on the lower shore 1m ACD;- 0.1m2/1mm mesh, and 0.01m2 over 0.5mm mesh. This habitat represents the 0.1m2 data. Abundant Nephtys hombergii were present in the infauna (entered as box quadrat). NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

The sediment comprised of coarse material largely shingle and gravel with interspersed grey silty mud moving into an anoxic blackening mixture at 5 to 10cm depth. Macrofaunal content was largely Hediste diversicolor with a prominent presence of small worms probably marine oligocheates and cirratulids. There was a large amount of detrital material in the surficial layers. One 0.1 m2 core was taken (entered as box quadrat). NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

The sediment comprised of coarse material largely shingle and gravel with interspersed grey silty mud moving into an anoxic blackening mixture at 5 to 10cm depth. Macrofaunal content was largely Hediste diversicolor with a prominent presence of small worms probably marine oligocheates and cirratulids. There was a large amount of detrital material in the surficial layers. One 0.1 m2 core was taken (entered as box quadrat). NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Predominently muddy shell gravel with stones at 3.3m ACD. An area of 0.1m2 excavated to 20cm and sieved over 1mm mesh (coarse material discarded, 1 Carcinus maenus not preserved) 0.01m2 dug to 10cm and sieved over a 0.5mm mesh. The infauna consisted of superabundant Hediste diversicolor and abundant oligochaetes and Abra alba. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Two cores taken here at 2.5m ACD; 0.1m2/1mm mesh and 0.01m2/0.5mm mesh. Anoxic silty mud. Black layer at shallow depth (around 5cm). Small numbers of whole shells cover the smooth surface (largely Scrobicularia plana). The sediment is glutinous with deep sink. Large numbers of Arenicola casts were visible and bait digging activities nearby indicated the presence of large indigenous populations. Macrofaunal content of cores was polycheate dominated, - mainly Hediste diversicolor which was superabundant. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.
Mixture of coarse gravel, sand, stones and quartzite granules with a few worms visible as macrofauna. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Fine sediment consisting of a thick layer of anoxic silty mud with large quantities of detrital material and a shallow rpd layer. Numbers of Nephtys hombergii in the sample were high along with lots of small worms. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Silty anoxic mud with some coarse gravel, quartzite granules and large twigs. A thick layer of detritus was also found. Polychaetes were dominant. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.
Thick black anoxic mud at 4.6 m bcd with high numbers of oligochaetes. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Very coarse sediment with broken slates and assorted stones, small cobbles and pebbles at 10.2 m bcd. The sediment contained many polychaetes with a very large count of Apseudes latreillii. Encrusting growth was found on the slates accounting for the presence of Pomatoceros lamarckii, Verruca stroemia, Dynamena pumila and dwarf Metridium senile. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Thick silty mud with black anoxic layer and some small encrusted stones at 8 m bcd. The black anoxic layer was at a very shallow depth and the sediment was dominated by polychaetes. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Anoxic silty mud at 6 m bcd with occasional stones and pebbles with detritus. Anaitides mucosa, Capitella capitata and oligochaetes were very numerous. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Anoxic silty mud with clay lumps (fine clay particles compacted together) and occasional stones and pebbles at 5.4 m bcd. High counts of Malacoceros fuliginosus and Caulleriella sp. were encountered. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Anoxic silty mud with occasional stones and pebbles with lots of shell gravel at 2.9 m bcd. High numbers of Melinna palmata were collected. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Coarse sand and shell gravel containing small shells at 6.8 m bcd. This site was the only dredge site to contain Nephtys cirrosa aswell as several crustaceans which were not found elsewhere in the estuary; Pontocrates arenarius, Megaluropus agilis and Atylus falcatus. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Upward facing bedrock in the kelp forest at 1.5m BCD. Smooth rock slope at area surveyed but broken slate elsewhere. Dominated by encrusting brown algae 40% of which was overgrown by Balanus crenatus and about 5% by encrusting bryozoa. Few foliose algae but dense Laminaria hyperborea overall. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Vertical and overhanging bedrock including gullies at 1.5 to 3.5m BCD. Dominated by orange encrusting bryozoa with ascidians common and a few erect bryozoa and hydrozoa. Algae were common in places - particularpy Corallina officinalis, Delesseria sanguinea and Dilsea carnosa. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Scoured bedrock adjacent to sand and cobbles at 5m BCD. Bare rock (and rock dominated by encrusting Corallinaceae) here had been colonised by Audouinella sp and Ectocarpoidea with Laminaria saccharina sporelings and common Asterias rubens on the rock. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Slate bedrock with some small overhangs at 9m BCD. Bonnemaisonia asparagoides, Brongniartella byssoides, Dilsea carnosa, Plocamium cartilagineum, Polyneura hilliae, Radicilingua thysanorhizans, Rhodophyta indet, Schottera nicaeensis and Dictyota dichotoma were all frequent. Of the animals Hemimycale columella, Haliclona viscosa, Caryophyllia smithii, Marthasterias glacialis, Echinus esculentus and Labrus bergylta were likewise frequent in their abundance. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Patches of stones and pebbles in gravel at 9m BCD. Dilsea carnosa, Grateloupia filicina, Peyssonelia sp, Laminaria saccharina, Sphacelaria sp, Enteromorpha sp, and Audouinella sp were all frequent. Few animals recorded here only Pomatoceros was frequently recorded. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Lower infralittoral broken bedrock at 2.7 to 4.2m BCD. Thick sticky mud in hollows, and muddy rock surfaces. Fairly wide variety of scattered algae, including Stenogramme interrupta unusually on bedrock. Laminaria saccharina was rare. There was no L. hyperborea. Several characteristic silt tolerent species (Phyllophora crispa, P. pseudoceranoides, Cordylecladia erecta). Laminaria saccharina fronds loaded with Celleporella sp. Several fish - Goldsinnys and Scorpion fish. Many large sponges on ridges. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Stones and small boulders on top of silt at 0.3m ASL. Heavy cover of Laminaria saccharina and other foliose algae, with many encrustig bryozoans. Verruca abundant on stones, and Pomatoceros at end of headland. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Patchy Zostera, dense in places, with much Laminaria saccharina mixed in particulary in shallow water. Much foliose algae as drift around Zostera bases, and also attached. Chorda and Gracilaria were frequent and Chondria rare. Epiphytes on Zostera are noted under habitat 4. Cereus was frequent in sediment, and Hinia and Sand gobies frequent on the surface, otherwise few animals were seen. Some holes in the sediment were observed. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Sublittoral fringe mooring blocks and chains. Luxuriant weed growth on older mooring blocks with Laminaria saccharina most evident, with Ulva sp. Ceramium rubrum, Cystoclonium purpureum and Desmarestia aculeata all large and frequent. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Steep very broken rock with rockmills, crevices, steep slopes and horizontal tops with abundant Laminaria hyperborea and foliose algae undercover in places, Corallina sp was dominant but overall was common. Delesseria, Callophyllis and Cryptopleura were common on the tops and Phyllophora and Polyneura were common on the steep sides of the rock. Encrusting orange bryozoans were abundant on steep faces especially in rockmills. There was heavy encrusting fauna on many algae. Colonial ascidians were common, Botrylloides and Morchellium were most common and of the sponges Hymeniacidon was frequent. Alcyonium and Caryophillia were occasional. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Here the Laminaria hyperborea was frequent whilst Laminaria saccharina was common. Encrusting dark red algae were abundant and pink encrusting algae were common, Ahnfeltia, Phyllophora, Polyneura, Callophyllis and Phyllophora were frequent. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Sand, pebble and cobble plain at 4 to 6m BCD. Here Cerianthus, Asterias, Gobius, Cancer, Pagurus and pink encrusting algae were all common. Lanice, Pomatoceros, Terebellidae indet, Ahnfeltia, Rhodomela, Phymatolithon and Laminaria saccharina were all recorded as frequent here. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Anoxic silty mud with occasional stones and pebbles with lots of shell gravel at 2.9 m bcd. High numbers of Melinna palmata were collected. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Mid shore and upper mid shore at 3 to 5m ACD. Steeply sloping slate broken in places. Dominated by limpets overgrown by Porphyra at the time of survey and with Fucus vesiculosus evesiculosus present. NOTE: Subsmtratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Lower mid shore at 2 to 3m ACD. Broken slate dominated by Fucus serratus with Lomentaria articulata, Palmaria palmata and other algae. Few barnacles present except for large numbers of Balanus perfoliata and animal species including common Dynamena pumila. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Middle shore broken bedrock with many rock ridges and small rockpools. Dominated by Porphyra with extensive cover, and Enteromorpha. A few fucoids (Fucus vesiculosus and F. serratus) in flatter areas, and patches of flat algal turf in damper areas and towards the lower middle shore. Barnacles were frequent (Chthamalus montagui, C. stellatus, Elminius modestus), with Semibalanus balanoides and Patella vulgata super abundant. Monodonta lineata, Actinia equina, Littorina menae and Hyale nilssoni were all frequent, and Gibbula umbilicalis occasional. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

location_name: NE of Royal Fowey Yacht Club (Fowey)
sample_comment: Underside of slipway to yacht club, 1 to 2m ACD. The upper levels were dominated by Elminius modestus with strewn Enteromorpha and Ulva. Lower levels had strewn Fucus seratus with ectocarpoids abundant, and a macrofaunal presence of Balanus perforatus dominant with occasional E. modestus. Attached to the algae were abundant Dynamena pumila and Alcyonidium gelatinosum. Attached to the rock were abundant Balanus perforatus, littorinids and spirorbids. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

location_name: NE of Royal Fowey Yacht Club (Fowey)
sample_comment: Sea wall 2.5 to 3m ACD. At 2.5m Balanus perforatus forms a dominant narrow band moving into a zone above of Elminius modestus, Chthamalus stellatus and Semibalanus balanoides with Patella spp. upto the high water mark. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.
Middle of cave. Dominated by Verruca stroemia with common encrusting Rhodophyta. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Lower shore boulders from ferry jetty to bedrock. All boulders were dominated by Fucus serratus, but cobbles and pebbles were chiefly bare with occasional cobbles covered in Enteromorpha sp and Ulva sp. Gigartina stellata was common on sides and under fucoids. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Lower shore bedrock and concrete slip. Thin area was Fucus serratus dominated but with a greater variety and abundance of red algae than on the boulders. Gelidium pusillum, Laurencia pinnatifida and encrusting Rhodophyta were most common here. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Mid shore steeply sloping broken bedrock including vertical surfaces dominated by Ascophyllum and Fucus serratus with some large patches of rock dominated by barnacles and Patella. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.
Lower mid shore broken rock dominated by Fucus serratus, heavily epiphyted with Alcyonidium gelatinosum. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Midshore bedrock. Dominated on steeply sloping and west facing bedrock planes by barnacles and limpets and on flats and sheltered slopes by Ascophyllum nodosum and fucoids chiefly Fucus serratus but some F. vesiculosus patches higher. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.
Lower mid shore and lower shore bedrock, dominated by Fucus serratus. A few areas limpet/barnacle dominated. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.
Lower shore dominated by Fucus serratus, and red and green algae. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Lower shore bedrock, including overhangs. Large cover of Fucus serratus, with Ceramium rubrum becoming predominent at low water level (0.7m) and lesser abundance of a variety of small red and green algae. Dynamena, Laomedea and Alcyonidium were again abundant on fucoids. Balanus crenatus was common and Elminius frequent, while Pomatoceros was common. Abundant amphipods and frequent Carcinus were present under fucoids. Bowerbankia imbricata was common and Hymeniacidon frequent. Occasional Asterias were present. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Under boulders in pools at ca. 1.6m ACD. Gibbula cineraria was super abundant and Cirripedia indet, and Patella were abundant. Fucus sp, Actinia, Porcellana, Umbonula and Botrylloides were all frequent. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Upper circalittoral broken bedrock, not so many overhangs as habitats 2 and 3. A few algae (Polyneura laciniata, Myriogramme heterocarpum) at 5.6m BCD; many more at 4.6m BCD, but still typical upper circalittoral species mainly. Animals turfs generally dominant - hydroids, bryozoans and large sponges including Suberites. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Very coarse sediment with broken slates and assorted stones, small cobbles and pebbles at 10.2 m bcd. The sediment contained many polychaetes with a very large count of Apseudes latreillii. Encrusting growth was found on the slates accounting for the presence of Pomatoceros lamarckii, Verruca stroemia, Dynamena pumila and dwarf Metridium senile. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Thick silty mud with black anoxic layer and some small encrusted stones at 8 m bcd. The black anoxic layer was at a very shallow depth and the sediment was dominated by polychaetes. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Coarse sand and shell gravel containing small shells at 6.8 m bcd. This site was the only dredge site to contain Nephtys cirrosa aswell as several crustaceans which were not found elsewhere in the estuary; Pontocrates arenarius, Megaluropus agilis and Atylus falcatus. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Broken bedrock at c.2m BCD. Overhangs, small caves and potholes - more silty than the main SE facing habitat and with some different species. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.
Overhangs on the lower shore wiqth a wide variety of species present including large areas of Umbonula littoralis and common Grantia compressa.

Middle shore and lower middle shore crevices had Hymeniacidon, Carcinus and a few Actinia equina, Littorina littorea, Alcyonium mytili, Cryptosula pallasiana and Bowerbankia imbricata. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Middle shore under overhangs. Barnacles, Elminius and Balanus crenatus were frequent, as were Littorina obtusata/mariae and encrusting bryozoans. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

location_name: NE of Royal Fowey Yacht Club (Fowey)
sample_comment: Sediment shore. Lower shore at 0.6m ACD. Coarse sand gravel (1 to 2mm) with overlying strewn shells (whole largely Venerupis) and small cobbles and pebbles and occasional large slates. Strewn Enteromorpha/Ulva were present in isolated clumps. A Lanice conchilega bed of 5 to 10/0.1m2 was present upto 1m ACD. No other obvious macrofauna within sediment cores. (0.1m2/0.01m2 sediment cores taken). This habitat represents the 0.1m2 data (entered as box quadrat). NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Sediment coarse sand mixed with fine (poorly sorted) stones and pebbles interspersed increasingly frequent lower down the shore. No visible macrofaunal community presence noted here within the sediment sampled. 0.1 m2 core revealed common nematodes and Gammarus salinus(entered as box quadrat). NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Mixture of coarse sand and gravel much the same as site D1. Worms again constituted the majority of the macrofauna with Pygospio elegans occuring in relatively high numbers when compared to the other fauna. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Fine sediment consisting of a thick layer of anoxic silty mud with large quantities of detrital material and a shallow rpd layer. Numbers of Nephtys hombergii in the sample were high along with lots of small worms. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Silty anoxic mud with some coarse gravel, quartzite granules and large twigs. A thick layer of detritus was also found. Polychaetes were dominant. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.
Thick black anoxic mud at 4.6 m bcd with high numbers of oligochaetes. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Very coarse sediment with broken slates and assorted stones, small cobbles and pebbles at 10.2 m bcd. The sediment contained many polychaetes with a very large count of Apseudes latreillii. Encrusting growth was found on the slates accounting for the presence of Pomatoceros lamarckii, Verruca stroemia, Dynamena pumila and dwarf Metridium senile. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Thick silty mud with black anoxic layer and some small encrusted stones at 8 m bcd. The black anoxic layer was at a very shallow depth and the sediment was dominated by polychaetes. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Anoxic silty mud at 6 m bcd with occasional stones and pebbles with detritus. Anaitides mucosa, Capitella capitata and oligochaetes were very numerous. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Anoxic silty mud with clay lumps (fine clay particles compacted together) and occasional stones and pebbles at 5.4 m bcd. High counts of Malacoceros fuliginosus and Caulleriella sp. were encountered. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Anoxic silty mud with occasional stones and pebbles with lots of shell gravel at 2.9 m bcd. High numbers of Melinna palmata were collected. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Algae as substrate. Littorina mariae, and Cladophora sp. were frequent. Ceramium rubrum, Porphyra sp. and filamentous Phaeophyta indet were common. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Lower shore. Broken bedrock with heavy algal cover of Fucus serratus with large amounts Laurencia pinnatifida, Gigartina stellata, Palmaria palmata and encrusting coralline algae. Laminaria digitata was abundant below. Balanus perforatus was common, with a variety of other animals occasional or frequent. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

location_name: NE of Royal Fowey Yacht Club (Fowey)
sample_comment: Sea wall 2.5 to 3m ACD. At 2.5m Balanus perforatus forms a dominant narrow band moving into a zone above of Elminius modestus, Chthamalus stellatus and Semibalanus balanoides with Patella spp. upto the high water mark. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Lower shore boulders from ferry jetty to bedrock. All boulders were dominated by Fucus serratus, but cobbles and pebbles were chiefly bare with occasional cobbles covered in Enteromorpha sp and Ulva sp. Gigartina stellata was common on sides and under fucoids. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Lower shore algae as substrate. Algae were heavily encrusted in hydroids and bryozoans particulary Alcyonidium gelatinosum, Dynamena pumila, Sertularella polyzonias and Flustrellidra hispida. Algal epiphytes were chiefly Porphyra sp and filamentous phaeophyta with Palmaria palmata, Lomentaria articulata, Ceramium rubrum and Ulva sp. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Lower shore bedrock and concrete slip. Thin area was Fucus serratus dominated but with a greater variety and abundance of red algae than on the boulders. Gelidium pusillum, Laurencia pinnatifida and encrusting Rhodophyta were most common here. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Overhang along concrete slip. This was overhung by Fucus serratus and the animal community was quite varied with sponges and ascidians, hydroids and bryozoans. Alcyonidium gelatinosum was abundant as were Pomatoceros sp. Balanus crenatus, and Dynamena pumila. Asterias rubens, Sertularia polyzonias, Halichondria panicea and Umbonula littoralis were frequent, and Leucosolenia botryoides, Botrylloides leachii, Clavelina lepadiformis, Morchellium argus and Sidnyum turbinatum were occasional. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.
Upper mid shore bedrock slope dominated by Ascophyllum with scattered barnacles and Patella below. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Mid shore steeply sloping broken bedrock including vertical surfaces dominated by Ascophyllum and Fucus serratus with some large patches of rock dominated by barnacles and Patella. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.
Lower mid shore broken rock dominated by Fucus serratus, heavily epiphyted with Alcyonidium gelatinosum. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.
Lower shore broken bedrock dominated by Fucus serratus with common Balanus crenatus on rock. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.
Lower mid shore and lower shore bedrock, dominated by Fucus serratus. A fecw areas limpet/barnacle dominated. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.
Lower shore dominated by Fucus serratus, and red and green algae. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Algae as substrate. Polysiphonia was abundant. Cystoclonium, Hypoglossum, Lomentaria, Palmaria, Enteromorpha, Dynamena, Sertularella and Alcyonidium were all frequent. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Lower middle shore at 2m ACD as gently sloping bedrock. Heavy cover of Ascophyllum, with Fucus serratus occasional. Gelidium pusillum common, and a small variety of red and green algae frequent or occasional. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Middle shore. Ascophyllum covering a wide zone from 2 to 4m with dense cover. A few patches not covered with Ascophyllum were dominated by limpets and barnacles, mainly Elminius. Frequent Fucus serratus and occasional F. vesiculosus were also present in the fucoid cover, with green algae frequent. Animals included abundant Littorinam ariae, Dynamena pumila, Laomedea flexuosa and frequent Clava multicornis. Bowerbankia imbricata was common, with a small variety of other species. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Lower middle shore on broken bedrock with heavy Fucus serratus cover. Gigartina stellata, Gelidium pusillum and Ulva sp were frequent. Enteromorpha was common along with a small variety of other red algae. Bowerbankia imbricata, Laomedea flexuosa and Dynamena pumhila were abundant, and amphipods and Alcyonidium gelatinosum common. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Lower shore bedrock. Similar to habitat 4, but with more frequent red algae including common Ceramium rubrum and lesser amounts of Antithamnion, Hypoglossum, Palmaria, Bryopsis plumosa and others. Large masses of Hymeniacidon near low water with abundant Balanus crenatus. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Lower midshore overhangs (animals only). Bowerbankia and Cryptosula were abundant, Semibalanus and Patella were frequent. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Wide midshore band dominated by Ascophyllum on broken bedrock with many steep and overhanging surfaces. Fucus serratus and F. vesiculosus occasional. F. vesiculosus was the only fucoid on a large metal mooring loop. A small variety of red and green algae were recorded. Dynamena pumila and Laomedea flexuosa were super abundant on large brown algae. Semibalanus balanoides were common and Elminius modestus frequent; Patella vulgata and Littorina mariae common. Abundant amphipods and Carcinus maenus were recorded, and Clava multicornis was frequent. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Lower midshore on broken bedrock with Fucus serratus dominant, and a small variety of small red algae. Ascophyllum was still frequent at this level, and green algae were common. Dynamena and Laomedea were both abundant on fucoids, and Clava frequent. Amphipods were abundant under fucoids, and Carcinus and Pomatoceros common. Littorina mariae was frequent and Littorina littorea was rare. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Lower shore bedrock, including overhangs. Large cover of Fucus serratus, with Ceramium rubrum becoming predominent at low water level (0.7m) and lesser abundance of a variety of small red and green algae. Dynamena, Laomedea and Alcyonidium were again abundant on fucoids. Balanus crenatus was common and Elminius frequent, while Pomatoceros was common. Abundant amphipods and frequent Carcinus were present under fucoids. Bowerbankia imbricata was common and Hymeniacidon frequent. Occasional Asterias were present. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Species recorded on algae from habitats 3 to 5. Alcyonidium was abundant. Ulva, Enteromorpha and polysiphonia were frequent. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Animals and algae on fucoids included Dynamena pumila, Laomedea, Alcyonium gelatinosum and Bowerbankia imbricata. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.
Middle shore. Animals recorded on algae were Alcyonidium gelatinosum and Bowerbankia imbricata. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Middle shore broken bedrock with heavy cover of Ascophyllum. A few Fucus vesiculosus at upper limits of Ascophyllum. Polysiphonia linosa was more abundant on the lowest Ascophyllum and absent at the top. Audouinella, Gelidium and Hildenbrandia were frequent. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Algae as substrate. Anemonia viridis, Alcyonidium gelatinosum, Electra pilosa, Ceramium rubrum and Ulva sp. were all frequent. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Pools at ca. 5m ACD, in steeply sloping slate bedrock. Mytilus edulis, Enteromorpha and rhodophyta indet were all abundant. Actinia and Patella were both frequent. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Pools at ca. 4.5m ACD (partly in deep shade). Cladophora sp., Corallina officinalis and rhodophyta indet were all abundant. Filamentous brown algae and Grantia compressa were both frequent. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Pools at 1.6m ACD. Fucus serratus was abundant. Dumontia, Gigartina, Laurencia(2 spp), Laminaria digitata and Osteichthyes indet were frequent. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Upper shore at 5 to 6m ACD. Steeply sloping slate broken in places. Dominated by barnacles with common Patella vulgata and many littorinids. NOTE: Substratum % data are convertde from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Mid shore and upper mid shore at 3 to 5m ACD. Steeply sloping slate broken in places. Dominated by limpets overgrown by Porphyra at the time of survey and with Fucus vesiculosus evesiculosus present. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Lower mid shore at 2 to 3m ACD. Broken slate odminated by Fucus serratus with Lomentaria articulata, Palmaria palmata and other algae. Pools at 3m ACD, amongst steeply sloping broken slate bedrock. Fucus serratus was abundant, similary rhodophyta ndet. Mastocarpus stellatus was frequent and Palmaria palmata was common. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Pools at ca.1.5m to ca.50m from point. Corallina officinalis and rhodophyta indet were abundant. Fucus serratus, Himanthalia elongata and Mastocarpus stellatus were all present. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Algae as substrate, within rock pools with no height band on shore given on recording sheets. Electra pilosa was frequent. Ceramium rubrum and Palmaria palmata were common. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Vertical bedrock in the sublittoral fringe at 1m ACD. Dominated by Chondrus crispus and Corallina officinalis with occasional Laminaria digitata. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Scoured bedrock adjacent to sand and cobbles at 5m BCD. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Middle shore broken bedrock with many rock ridges and small rockpools. Dominated by Porphyra with extensive cover, and Enteromorpha. A few fucoids (Fucus vesiculosus and F. serratus) in flatter areas, and patches of flat algal turf in damper areas and towards the lower middle shore. Barnacles were frequent (Chthamalus montagui, C. stellatus, Elminius modestus), with Semibalanus balanoides and Patella vulgata super abundant. Monodonta lineata, Actinia equina, Littorina menae and Hyale nilssoni were all frequent, and Gibbula umbilicalis occasional. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Midshore bedrock. Dominated on steeply sloping and west facing bedrock planes by barnacles and limpets and on flats and sheltered slopes by Ascophyllum nodosum and fucoids chiefly Fucus serratus but some F. vesiculosus patches higher. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Lower middle shore on broken bedrock with heavy Fucus serratus cover. Gigartina stellata, Gelidium pusillum and Ulva sp were frequent. Lower infralittoral broken bedrock at 2.7 to 4.2m BCD. Thick sticky mud in hollows, and muddy rock surfaces. Several characteristic silt tolerent species (Phyllophora crispa, P. pseudoceranoides, Cordylecladia erecta). Laminaria saccharina fronds loaded with Celleporella sp. Several fish - Goldsinnys and Scorpion fish. Many large sponges on ridges. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Sand, pebble and cobble plain at 4 to 6m BCD. Here Cerianthus, Asterias, Gobius, Cancer, Pagurus and pink encrusting algae were all common. Lanice, Pomatoceros, Terebellidae indet, Ahnfeltia, Rhodomela, Phymatolithon and Laminaria saccharina were all recorded as frequent here. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Sand, pebble and cobble plain at 4 to 6m BCD. Here Cerianthus, Asterias, Gobius, Cancer, Pagurus and pink encrusting algae were all common. Algae as substratum, with frequent Palmaria and Porphyra and common Phycodrys. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Algae as substrate. Obelia geniculata and Membranipora membranecea were abundant, Porphyra sp. and Cryptopleura ramosa were recorded as frequent. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Midshore overhanging rock near cave entrance with scattered barnacles and Actinia equina and abundant Plumularia elegans. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Middle of cave. Dominated by Verruca stroemia with common encrusting Rhodophyta. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Lower shore boulders from ferry jetty to bedrock. Gigartina stellata was common on sides and under fucoids. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Infralittoral bedrock at 0 to 1.5m BCD. Broken rock platform dominated by filamentous algae (20 - 60%) and red foliose algae in upper part foliose more abundant 1 to 1.5m. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Vertical or overhanging bedrock 3 to 5m dominated by a turf of Bugula turbinata and Scrupocellaria with sponges and hydroids over rock covered by tubiculous amphipods. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

location_name: NE of Royal Fowey Yacht Club (Fowey)
sample_comment: Sediment shore. Lower shore at 0.6m ACD. Strewn Enteromorpha/Ulva were present in isolated clumps. A Lanice conchilega bed of 5 to 10/0.1m2 was present upto 1m ACD. No other obvious macrofauna within sediment cores. (0.1m2/0.01m2 sediment cores taken). This habitat represents the 0.1m2 data (entered as box quadrat). NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Silty anoxic mud with some coarse gravel, quartzite granules and large twigs. A thick layer of detritus was also found. Polychaetes were dominant. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.
Thick black anoxic mud at 4.6 m bcd with high numbers of oligochaetes. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Very coarse sediment with broken slates and assorted stones, small cobbles and pebbles at 10.2 m bcd. The sediment contained many polychaetes with a very large count of Apseudes latreillii. Encrusting growth was found on the slates accounting for the presence of Pomatoceros lamarckii, Verruca stroemia, Dynamena pumila and dwarf Metridium senile. Drift dive over sediment surface at 7m BCD. Rippled coarse sand sediment with a significant shell gravel content. The sediment showed many signs of burrowing activity with a prominent presence of Ensis ensis (siphons visible, also empty shells on surface), Bivalve siphons indet, Lanice conchylega (C), Terebellidae indet (large Lanice- like), Echinocardium cordatum (some empty casts on the surface). Occasional Cerianthus lloydii. Also Sagartiogeton undatus and Urticina felina were found as solitary individuals. Large numbers of drogonets Callionymus lyra were seen together with Gobidae indet (various sand gobies). Also large numbers of Asterias rubens and Carcinus maenas were seen on the surface. The surface was strewn with various sized mobile cobbles the larger ones being covered by Laminaria saccharina with attached hydroid and bryozoan growth. Smaller stones were largely colonised by Pomatoceros. Several fish species were noteably present especially Plaice and Pollack. Pleuronectes platessa was in the water column. sample_comment: Underside of slipway to yacht club, 1 to 2m ACD. The upper levels were dominated by Elminius modestus with strewn Enteromorpha and Ulva. Upper circalittoral broken bedrock, not so many overhangs as habitats 2 and 3. A few algae (Polyneura laciniata, Myriogramme heterocarpum) at 5.6m BCD; many more at 4.6m BCD, but still typical upper circalittoral species mainly. Animals turfs generally dominant - hydroids, bryozoans and large sponges including Suberites. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Upper cricalittoral bedrock 1.5 to 3m BCD. Upward facing rock ledges dominated by tube dwelling amphipods and Maldanidae? with Suberites domuncula. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Vertical or overhanging bedrock 3 to 5m dominated by a turf of Bugula turbinata and Scrupocellaria with sponges and hydroids over rock covered by tubiculous amphipods. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Infralittoral rock spur at 0 to 2m BCD. Broken rock with extensive vertical, overhanging and upward facing rock dominated by tubiculous amphipods, with scattered foliose algae on upward facing surfaces and by Bugula turbinata and various hydroids on vertical surfaces. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

location_name: No. 3 Quay, S of Upper Carn Point (Fowey)
sample_comment: Steel jetty piles ca 1.5m diameter at ca 3 to 5.5m, dominated by Bugula turbinata with tubiculous amphipods below and scattered Ascidiella aspersa, hydroids and sponges and other ascidians. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Under stones in stream near Penpoll Bridge. Here Anguilla and Elminius were frequent. Cymodoce and amphipods were common. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.
Mud flats of shaley mud with some small boulders. Fucus ceranoides was frequent. Amphipoda indet was common. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.
Lower shore dominated by Fucus serratus, and red and green algae. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Verical rockwall in sublittoral fringe at 0 to 0.5m BCD. Dominated by Corallina which was almost 100% cover. Chondrus was frequent. Phorbas fictitius? and Bugula plumosa were also frequent. Haliclystus was abundant. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Bedrock overhangs in sublittoral fringe at 0 to 0.5m BCD. Here an interesting encrusting faunal growth was noted including Halichondria panicea, Myxilla incrustans, Scypha ciliata, Corynactis viridis, dwarf Metridium senile and Botrylloides leachi. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Upward facing bedrock in the kelp forest at 1.5m BCD. Smooth rock slope at area surveyed but broken slate elsewhere. Dominated by encrusting brown algae 40% of which was overgrown by Balanus crenatus and about 5% by encrusting bryozoa. Few foliose algae but dense Laminaria hyperborea overall. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Scoured bedrock adjacent to sand and cobbles at 5m BCD. Bare rock (and rock dominated by encrusting Corallinaceae) here had been colonised by Audouinella sp and Ectocarpoidea with Laminaria saccharina sporelings and common Asterias rubens on the rock. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Verical rockwall in sublittoral fringe at 0 to 0.5m BCD. Dominated by Corallina which was almost 100% cover. Chondrus was frequent. Phorbas fictitius? and Bugula plumosa were also frequent. Haliclystus was abundant. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.
Sublittoral fringe steep bedrock near sand at 1m BCD. Dominated by encrusting reds and pinks. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.
Algae as substrate in sublittoral fringe at about 0 to 1m BCD. Haliclystus auricula was abundant. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Dense algae on firm muddy sand. Abundant Laminaria saccharina and common Ceramium rurum and Ulva and a variety of others frequent. Few animals were recorded, mainly encrusting bryozoans on algal fronds. Caprellids, Mysids were frequent. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Vertical and overhanging bedrock including gullies at 1.5 to 3.5m BCD. Dominated by orange encrusting bryozoa with ascidians common and a few erect bryozoa and hydrozoa. Algae were common in places - particulary Corallina officinalis, Delesseria sanguinea and Dilsea carnosa. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Sublittoral fringe mooring blocks and chains. Luxuriant weed growth on older mooring blocks with Laminaria saccharina most evident, with Ulva sp. Ceramium rubrum, Cystoclonium purpureum and Desmarestia aculeata all large and frequent. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Vertical and overhanging bedrock including gullies at 1.5 to 3.5m BCD. Dominated by orange encrusting bryozoa with ascidians common and a few erect bryozoa and hydrozoa. Algae were common in places - particulary Corallina officinalis, Delesseria sanguinea and Dilsea carnosa. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Slate bedrock with some small overhangs at 9m BCD. Bonnemaisonia asparagoides, Brongniartella byssoides, Dilsea carnosa, Plocamium cartilagineum, Polyneura hilliae, Radicilingua thysanorhizans, Rhodophyta indet, Schottera nicaeensis and Dictyota dichotoma were all frequent. Of the animals Hemimycale columella, Haliclona viscosa, Caryophyllia smithii, Marthasterias glacialis, Echinus esculentus and Labrus bergylta were likewise frequent in their abundance. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Sublittoral fringe bedrock at 0.6m ACD. Surveyed mainly for algae. Scattered plants of the usual fringe species, with Ceramium rubrum common. Prosthecaraeus vittatus and Clavelina lepadiformis on overhangs. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Upper circalittoral broken bedrock, not so many overhangs as habitats 2 and 3. A few algae (Polyneura laciniata, Myriogramme heterocarpum) at 5.6m BCD; many more at 4.6m BCD, but still typical upper circalittoral species mainly. Animals turfs generally dominant - hydroids, bryozoans and large sponges including Suberites. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Vertical or overhanging bedrock 3 to 5m dominated by a turf of Bugula turbinata and Scrupocellaria with sponges and hydroids over rock covered by tubiculous amphipods. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Sublittoral fringe ledges and overhangs at 0.5 to 0m BCD. Silty with a turf of ?Ceramium rubrum and scattered foliose red algae on upward facing surfaces and a small variety of animals, most conspicuously Clavellina lepadiformis and Leucosolenia botryoides. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Broken bedrock at +0.3m with heavy cover of foliose algae and Laminaria saccharina just below. Mainly Ceramium rubrum with a good variety of other algae. Animals sparse, except for encrusting bryozoans on algae and some sponges. Tiny prosobranchs Lacuna vincta collected with algae and probably abundant. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

location_name: No. 3 Quay, S of Upper Carn Point (Fowey)
sample_comment: Steel jetty piles ca 1.5m diameter at ca 3 to 5.5m, dominated by Bugula turbinata with tubiculous amphipods below and scattered Ascidiella aspersa, hydroids and ponges and other ascidians. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Slate bedrock with some small overhangs at 9m BCD. Bonnemaisonia asparagoides, Brongniartella byssoides, Dilsea carnosa, Plocamium cartilagineum, Polyneura hilliae, Radicilingua thysanorhizans, Rhodophyta indet, Schottera nicaeensis and Dictyota dichotoma were all frequent. Of the animals Hemimycale columella, Haliclona viscosa, Caryophyllia smithii, Marthasterias glacialis, Echinus esculentus and Labrus bergylta were likewise frequent in their abundance. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Two cores taken here at 2.5m ACD; 0.1m2/1mm mesh and 0.01m2/0.5mm mesh. Anoxic silty mud. Black layer at shallow depth (around 5cm). Small numbers of whole shells cover the smooth surface (largely Scrobicularia plana). The sediment is glutinous with deep sink. Large numbers of Arenicola casts were visible and bait digging activities nearby indicated the presence of large indigenous populations. Macrofaunal content of cores was polycheate dominated, - mainly Hediste diversicolor which was superabundant. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Mixture of coarse sand and gravel much the same as site D1. Worms again constituted the majority of the macrofauna with Pygospio elegans occuring in relatively high numbers when compared to the other fauna. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Lower shore steeply sloping slate at 0.7 to 1.5m ACD. Dominated by Laminaria digitata, Gigartina stellata and Palmaria palmata with Umbonula littoralis covering large areas of rock and scattered Grantia compressa. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Lower shore bedrock and concrete slip. Thin area was Fucus serratus dominated but with a greater variety and abundance of red algae than on the boulders. Gelidium pusillum, Laurencia pinnatifida and encrusting Rhodophyta were most common here. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Mid shore steeply sloping broken bedrock including vertical surfaces dominated by Ascophyllum and Fucus serratus with some large patches of rock dominated by barnacles and Patella. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.
Lower mid shore broken rock dominated by Fucus serratus, heavily epiphyted with Alcyonidium gelatinosum. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.
Lower shore broken bedrock dominated by Fucus serratus with common Balanus crenatus on rock. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Midshore bedrock. Dominated on steeply sloping and west facing bedrock planes by barnacles and limpets and on flats and sheltered slopes by Ascophyllum nodosum and fucoids chiefly Fucus serratus but some F. vesiculosus patches higher. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.
Lower mid shore and lower shore bedrock, dominated by Fucus serratus. A few areas limpet/barnacle dominated. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.
Lower shore dominated by Fucus serratus, and red and green algae. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Lower middle shore at 2m ACD as gently sloping bedrock. Heavy cover of Ascophyllum, with Fucus serratus occasional. Gelidium pusillum common, and a small variety of red and green algae frequent or occasional. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Lower middle shore on broken bedrock with heavy Fucus serratus cover. Gigartina stellata, Gelidium pusillum and Ulva sp were frequent. Enteromorpha was common along with a small variety of other red algae. Bowerbankia imbricata, Laomedea flexuosa and Dynamena pumila were abundant, and amphipods and Alcyonidium gelatinosum common. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substraum 1-3 scoring system.

Haliclystus was abundant. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original ubstratum 1-3 scoring system.
Sublittoral fringe steep bedrock near sand at 1m BCD. Dominated by encrusting reds and pinks. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Patches of stones and pebbles in gravel at 9m BCD. Dilsea carnosa, Grateloupia filicina, Peyssonelia sp, Laminaria saccharina, Sphacelaria sp, Enteromorpha sp, and Audouinella sp were all frequent. Few animals recorded here only Pomatoceros was frequently recorded. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Small boulders on mud at about 0m CD. Colonised by a variety of red algae (animals not recorded). NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Broken bedrock at +0.3m with heavy cover of foliose algae and Laminaria saccharina just below. Mainly Ceramium rubrum with a good variety of other algae. Animals sparse, except for encrusting bryozoans on algae and some sponges. Tiny prosobranchs Lacuna vincta collected with algae and probably abundant. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Ceramium rubrum, Cystoclonium purpureum and Desmarestia aculeata all large and frequent. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Steep very broken rock with rockmills, crevices, steep slopes and horizontal tops with abundant Laminaria hyperborea and foliose algae undercover in places, Corallina sp was dominant but overall was common. Delesseria, Callophyllis and Cryptopleura were common on the tops and Phyllophora and Polyneura were common on the steep sides of the rock. Encrusting orange bryozoans were abundant on steep faces especially in rockmills. There was heavy encrusting fauna on many algae. Colonial ascidians were common, Botrylloides and Morchellium were most common and of the sponges Hymeniacidon was frequent. Alcyonium and Caryophillia were occasional. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Sand plain with cobbles at 4m BCD. Ceramium, Cryptopleura, Desmarestia, Laminaria saccharina and Ulva were all frequent. Lanice and Terebellids were abundant. Hydractinia, Pomatoceros, Carcinus, Ensis and Callionymus were recorded as frequent. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Very coarse sediment with broken slates and assorted stones, small cobbles and pebbles at 10.2 m bcd. The sediment contained many polychaetes with a very large count of Apseudes latreillii. Encrusting growth was found on the slates accounting for the presence of Pomatoceros lamarckii, Verruca stroemia, Dynamena pumila and dwarf Metridium senile. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Broken bedrock at c.2m BCD. Overhangs, small caves and potholes - more silty than the main SE facing habitat and with some different species. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Lower infralittoral broken bedrock at 2.7 to 4.2m BCD. Thick sticky mud in hollows, and muddy rock surfaces. Fairly wide variety of scattered algae, including Stenogramme interrupta unusually on bedrock. Laminaria saccharina was rare. There was no L. hyperborea. Several characteristic silt tolerent species (Phyllophora crispa, P. pseudoceranoides, Cordylecladia erecta). Laminaria saccharina fronds loaded with Celleporella sp. Several fish - Goldsinnys and Scorpion fish. Many large sponges on ridges. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Boulders and stones in sediment at 8.5m BCD. Partly embedded with with tops colonised by hydroids, sponges (Leucosolenia sp) and orange encrusting bryozoans. One Ulva plant apparently attached. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Stones of vrious sizes at 8.5m BCD, overlying muddy sediment. Smaller clean and obviously mobile. Dominant animals were Pomatoceros sp and barnacles. A fairly wide variety of other animals typical of this habitat. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Sediment was a silty grey mud with a smooth surface and diatom film (brown) covering. No anoxic layer was seen down to the 10cm depth sampled. Two cores of sediment taken on the lower shore 1m ACD;- 0.1m2/1mm mesh, and 0.01m2 over 0.5mm mesh. This habitat represents the 0.1m2 data. Abundant Nephtys hombergii were present in the infauna (entered as box quadrat). NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Clayey mud at the base of the steep slope where mud slopes gradually to the pit at the entrance. 1 times 0.1m2 sieved over 1mm mesh. This habitat represents the 0.1 m2 box quadrat. Hediste diversicolor and Cirratulis cirratus were abundant as infauna. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Fine sediment consisting of a thick layer of anoxic silty mud with large quantities of detrital material and a shallow rpd layer. Silty anoxic mud with some coarse gravel, quartzite granules and large twigs. A thick layer of detritus was also found. Polychaetes were dominant. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.
Thick black anoxic mud at 4.6 m bcd with high numbers of oligochaetes. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Thick silty mud with black anoxic layer and some small encrusted stones at 8 m bcd. The black anoxic layer was at a very shallow depth and the sediment was dominated by polychaetes. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Anoxic silty mud at 6 m bcd with occasional stones and pebbles with detritus. Anaitides mucosa, Capitella capitata and oligochaetes were very numerous. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Anoxic silty mud with clay lumps (fine clay particles compacted together) and occasional stones and pebbles at 5.4 m bcd. High counts of Malacoceros fuliginosus and Caulleriella sp. were encountered. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Anoxic silty mud with occasional stones and pebbles with lots of shell gravel at 2.9 m bcd. High numbers of Melinna palmata were collected. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Silty anoxic mud with some coarse gravel, quartzite granules and large twigs. A thick layer of detritus was also found. Polychaetes were dominant. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Scoured bedrock adjacent to sand and cobbles at 5m BCD. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Quartz pebbles, cobbles and small boulders at 5.2m BCD. Asperococcus sp and Enteromorpha sp were common together with smaller numbers of colonising species. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Slate bedrock with some small overhangs at 9m BCD. Bonnemaisonia asparagoides, Brongniartella byssoides, Dilsea carnosa, Plocamium cartilagineum, Polyneura hilliae, Radicilingua thysanorhizans, Rhodophyta indet, Schottera nicaeensis and Dictyota dichotoma were all frequent. Of the animals Hemimycale columella, Haliclona viscosa, Caryophyllia smithii, Marthasterias glacialis, Echinus esculentus and Labrus bergylta were likewise frequent in their abundance. Patches of stones and pebbles in gravel at 9m BCD. Dilsea carnosa, Grateloupia filicina, Peyssonelia sp, Laminaria saccharina, Sphacelaria sp, Enteromorpha sp, and Audouinella sp were all frequent. Few animals recorded here only Pomatoceros was frequently recorded. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Upper infralittoral broken bedrock at 0.9 to 1.4m BCD. Fairly dense foliose algae on silty horizontal ledges, steep and overhanging faces with some animals, mainly turfs. Scattered very large sponges (green pipes) Ciocalypta penicillus. Polyneura hilliae abundant, Laminaria hyperborea rare, and Laminaria saccharina common. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Sublittoral fringe ledges and overhangs at 0.5 to 0m BCD. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Broken bedrock at +0.3m with heavy cover of foliose algae and Laminaria saccharina just below. Mainly Ceramium rubrum with a good variety of other algae. Stones and small boulders on top of silt at 0.3m ASL. Heavy cover of Laminaria saccharina and other foliose algae, with many encrusting bryozoans. Verruca abundant on stones, and Pomatoceros at end of headland. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Sublittoral fringe mooring blocks and chains. Luxuriant weed growth on older mooring blocks with Laminaria saccharina most evident, with Ulva sp. Sand, pebble and cobble plain at 4 to 6m BCD. Here Cerianthus, Asterias, Gobius, Cancer, Pagurus and pink encrusting algae were all common. Sand plain with cobbles at 4m BCD. Ceramium, Cryptopleura, Desmarestia, Laminaria saccharina and Ulva were all frequent. Lanice and Terebellids were abundant. Drift dive over sediment surface at 7m BCD. Rippled coarse sand sediment with a significant shell gravel content. The sediment showed many signs of burrowing activity with a prominent presence of Ensis ensis (siphons visible, also empty shells on surface), Bivalve siphons indet, Lanice conchylega (C), Terebellidae indet (large Lanice- like), Echinocardium cordatum (some empty casts on the surface). Occasional Cerianthus lloydii. Also Sagartiogeton undatus and Urticina felina were found as solitary individuals. Large numbers of drogonets Callionymus lyra were seen together with Gobidae indet (various sand gobies). Also large numbers of Asterias rubens and Carcinus maenas were seen on the surface. The surface was strewn with various sized mobile cobbles the larger ones being covered by Laminaria saccharina with attached hydroid and bryozoan growth. Smaller stones were largely colonised by Pomatoceros. Several fish species were noteably present especially Plaice and Pollack. Pleuronectes platessa was in the water column. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Vertical and overhanging bedrock including gullies at 1.5 to 3.5m BCD. Dominated by orange encrusting bryozoa with ascidians common and a few erect bryozoa and hydrozoa. Middle shore broken bedrock with many rock ridges and small rockpools. Dominated by Porphyra with extensive cover, and Enteromorpha. Midshore. Barnacle/limpet dominated. No plants were recorded here. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Animals turfs generally dominant - hydroids, bryozoans and large sponges including Suberites. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Boulders and stones in sediment at 8.5m BCD. Partly embedded with with tops colonised by hydroids, sponges (Leucosolenia sp) and orange encrusting bryozoans. One Ulva plant apparently attached. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Sediment was a silty grey mud with a smooth surface and diatom film (brown) covering. No anoxic layer was seen down to the 10cm depth sampled. Two cores of sediment taken on the lower shore 1m ACD;- 0.1m2/1mm mesh, and 0.01m2 over 0.5mm mesh. This habitat represents the 0.1m2 data. Abundant Nephtys hombergii were present in the infauna (entered as box quadrat). NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Small numbers of whole shells cover the smooth surface (largely Scrobicularia plana). The sediment is glutinous with deep sink. Large numbers of Arenicola casts were visible and bait digging activities nearby indicated the presence of large indigenous populations. Macrofaunal content of cores was polycheate dominated, - mainly Hediste diversicolor which was superabundant. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Algae as substrate. Anemonia viridis, Alcyonidium gelatinosum, Electra pilosa, Ceramium rubrum and Ulva sp. were all frequent. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.
Algae as substratum, with frequent Palmaria and Porphyra and common Phycodrys. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Silty anoxic mud with some coarse gravel, quartzite granules and large twigs. A thick layer of detritus was also found. Polychaetes were dominant. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Silty anoxic mud with some coarse gravel, quartzite granules and large twigs. A thick layer of detritus was also found. Polychaetes were dominant. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Very coarse sediment with broken slates and assorted stones, small cobbles and pebbles at 10.2 m bcd. The sediment contained many polychaetes with a very large count of Apseudes latreillii. Encrusting growth was found on the slates accounting for the presence of Pomatoceros lamarckii, Verruca stroemia, Dynamena pumila and dwarf Metridium senile. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Anoxic silty mud with occasional stones and pebbles with lots of shell gravel at 2.9 m bcd. High numbers of Melinna palmata were collected. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Sand plain with cobbles at 4m BCD. Ceramium, Cryptopleura, Desmarestia, Laminaria saccharina and Ulva were all frequent. Lanice and Terebellids were abundant. Hydractinia, Pomatoceros, Carcinus, Ensis and Callionymus were recorded as frequent. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Drift dive over sediment surface at 7m BCD. Rippled coarse sand sediment with a significant shell gravel content. The sediment showed many signs of burrowing activity with a prominent presence of Ensis ensis (siphons visible, also empty shells on surface), Bivalve siphons indet, Lanice conchylega (C), Terebellidae indet (large Lanice- like), Echinocardium cordatum (some empty casts on the surface). Occasional Cerianthus lloydii. Also Sagartiogeton undatus and Urticina felina were found as solitary individuals. Large numbers of drogonets Callionymus lyra were seen together with Gobidae indet (various sand gobies). Also large numbers of Asterias rubens and Carcinus maenas were seen on the surface. The surface was strewn with various sized mobile cobbles the larger ones being covered by Laminaria saccharina with attached hydroid and bryozoan growth. Smaller stones were largely colonised by Pomatoceros. Several fish species were noteably present especially Plaice and Pollack. Pleuronectes platessa was in the water column. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Slate bedrock with some small overhangs at 9m BCD. Bonnemaisonia asparagoides, Brongniartella byssoides, Dilsea carnosa, Plocamium cartilagineum, Polyneura hilliae, Radicilingua thysanorhizans, Rhodophyta indet, Schottera nicaeensis and Dictyota dichotoma were all frequent. Of the animals Hemimycale columella, Haliclona viscosa, Caryophyllia smithii, Marthasterias glacialis, Echinus esculentus and Labrus bergylta were likewise frequent in their abundance. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Beyond the Zostera bed, a flat featureless sediment plain with few algae and visible animals. Mollusc siphons were observed once. Cereus was frequent, Sagartiogeton occasional and Cerianthus was rare. Several hermit crabs were seen, one with Hydractinia. Plaice were occasional and Sand gobies frequent and one dragonet was seen. A small Tonicella was found lying on the sediment. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Mud plain and slope. On mud were occasaional Plaice, Flounders and Sand gobies but most abundant were hermit crabs (Paguridae indet), Nassarius reticulatus, Crangon crangon and Carcinus maenas. There were one or two Cerianthus lloydii. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Sand plain with cobbles at 4m BCD. Ceramium, Cryptopleura, Desmarestia, Laminaria saccharina and Ulva were all frequent. Lanice and Terebellids were abundant. Hydractinia, Pomatoceros, Carcinus, Ensis and Callionymus were recorded as frequent. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

The sediment showed many signs of burrowing activity with a prominent presence of Ensis ensis (siphons visible, also empty shells on surface), Bivalve siphons indet, Lanice conchylega (C), Terebellidae indet (large Lanice- like), Echinocardium cordatum (some empty casts on the surface). Smaller stones were largely colonised by Pomatoceros. Several fish species were noteably present especially Plaice and Pollack. Pleuronectes platessa was in the water column. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Pools at 1.6m ACD. Fucus serratus was abundant. Dumontia, Gigartina, Laurencia(2 spp), Laminaria digitata and Osteichthyes indet were frequent. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Middle shore broken bedrock with many rock ridges and small rockpools. Dominated by Porphyra with extensive cover, and Enteromorpha. A few fucoids (Fucus vesiculosus and F. serratus) in flatter areas, and patches of flat algal turf in damper areas and towards the lower middle shore. Barnacles were frequent (Chthamalus montagui, C. stellatus, Elminius modestus), with Semibalanus balanoides and Patella vulgata super abundant. Monodonta lineata, Actinia equina, Littorina menae and Hyale nilssoni were all frequent, and Gibbula umbilicalis occasional. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Lower shore bedrock and concrete slip. Thin area was Fucus serratus dominated but with a greater variety and abundance of red algae than on the boulders. Gelidium pusillum, Laurencia pinnatifida and encrusting Rhodophyta were most common here. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.
Lower shore dominated by Fucus serratus, and red and green algae. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Anoxic silty mud with clay lumps (fine clay particles compacted together) and occasional stones and pebbles at 5.4 m bcd. High counts of Malacoceros fuliginosus and Caulleriella sp. were encountered. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Infralittoral rock spur at 0 to 2m BCD. Broken rock with extensive vertical, overhanging and upward facing rock dominated by tubiculous amphipods, with scattered foliose algae on upward facing surfaces and by Bugula turbinata and various hydroids on vertical surfaces. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Rippled sand plain at 6m BCD. Bare rippled sand except for a few pagurids and Lanice but with a high density of bivalve siphons - most likely Ensis sp. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.
Mud with slate pebbles at 0 to 2m BCD. Most conspicuously colonised boy Sagartiogeton undatus. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Mud slope and hummocks with scattered quartz pebbles. Largely bare except for occasional Sagartiogeton undatus and mobile species. Reason for hummocked appearance not clear but could be dredging (hummocks run N - S). NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Quartz pebbles and cobbles on mud. A steep slope 2m high with area at the top covered by pebbles and cobbles swarming with small Pagurus. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Sand, pebble and cobble plain at 4 to 6m BCD. Here Cerianthus, Asterias, Gobius, Cancer, Pagurus and pink encrusting algae were all common. Lanice, Pomatoceros, Terebellidae indet, Ahnfeltia, Rhodomela, Phymatolithon and Laminaria saccharina were all recorded as frequent here. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Sand plain with cobbles at 4m BCD. Ceramium, Cryptopleura, Desmarestia, Laminaria saccharina and Ulva were all frequent. Lanice and Terebellids were abundant. Hydractinia, Pomatoceros, Carcinus, Ensis and Callionymus were recorded as frequent. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.
