Lower midshore on broken bedrock with Fucus serratus dominant, and a small ariety of small red algae. Ascophyllum was still frequent at this level, and green algae were common. Dynamena and Laomedea were both abundant on fucoids, and Clava frequent. Amphipods were abundant under fucoids, and Carcinus and Pomatoceros common. Littorina mariae was frequent and Littorina littorea was rare. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Lower middle shore, broken bedrock, dominated by Ascophyllum but also with Fucus vesiculosus and F. serratus. Gigartina stellata and Enteromorpha were common. Audouinella, Callithamnion, encrusting red algae and Cladophora rupestris were all frequent. Dynamena and Laomedea were both common on algae. Mytilus edulis, Littorina menae, Balanus crenatus and Bowerbankia imbricata all frequent, and one patch c. 1m2 of vertical bedrock had c. 200 limpets, and large barnacle cover. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Middle shore and lower middle shore crevices had Hymeniacidon, Carcinus and a few Actinia equina, Littorina littorea, Alcyonium mytili, Cryptosula pallasiana and Bowerbankia imbricata. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Under boulders on the lower middle shore. Carcinus and Terebellids were common, Elminius frequent. One Anguilla (juvenile) was recorded, and Littorina liittorea was occasional. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Upper shore including splash zone. Lichens on steep bedrock, the usual upper shore Pelvetia/Bostrychia and F. spiralis/Catenella on flatter rock and more extensive bands. Enteromorpha was also abundant at this level, as were amphipods. Red mites were frequent, as well as Littorina saxitilis and L. littorea. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Middle shore broken bedrock dominated by Ascophyllum with much epiphytic Polysiphonia lanosa and occasional Fucus vesiculosus. Gelidium pusillum was abundant and Mastocarpus stellatus was frequent under the Ascophyllum. Elminius was common and Balanus crenatus and Semibalanus balanoides were both frequent. Abundant amphipods and Littorina littorea were recorded, and frequent Carcinus. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Upper shore on steepish broken bedrock with fairly well developed lichens (shaded but not excessively so) and Pelvetia, Bostrychia and F. spiralis/Catenella bands. Hildenbrandia was common. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Middle shore broken bedrock with heavy cover of Ascophyllum. A few Fucus vesiculosus at upper limits of Ascophyllum. Polysiphonia linosa was more abundant on the lowest Ascophyllum and absent at the top. Audouinella, Gelidium and Hildenbrandia were frequent. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Middle shore under stones. Species recorded were amphipods (EX), Carcinus maenus (SA), Littorina littorea (F) and Mytilus edulis (C). NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Lower middle shore bedrock. Dominated with Ascophyllum as middle shore, but with Mastocarpus stellatus common beneath. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Lower mid shore and mid shore, on and under stones and boulders. Fucus vesiculosus abundant on muddy shale, with F. ceranoides at low water. Elachista was frequent on Fucus vesiculosus. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Patches of stones and pebbles in gravel at 9m BCD. Dilsea carnosa, Grateloupia filicina, Peyssonelia sp, Laminaria saccharina, Sphacelaria sp, Enteromorpha sp, and Audouinella sp were all frequent. Few animals recorded here only Pomatoceros was frequently recorded. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Lower mid shore at 2 to 3m ACD. Broken slate dominated by Fucus serratus with Lomentaria articulata, Palmaria palmata and other algae. Few barnacles present except for large numbers of Balanus perforliata and animal species including common Dynamena pumila. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Lower shore steeply sloping slate at 0.7 to 1.5m ACD. Dominated by Laminaria digitata, Gigartina stellata and Palmaria palmata with Umbonula littoralis covering large areas of rock and scattered Grantia compressa. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Pools at 3m ACD, amongst steeply sloping broken slate bedrock. Fucus serratus was abundant, similary rhodophyta indet. Mastocarpus stellatus was frequent and Palmaria palmata was common. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoirng system.

Pools at ca.1.5m to ca.50m from point. Corallina officinalis and rhodophyta indet were abundant. Fucus serratus, Himanthalia elongata and Mastocarpus stellatus were all present. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Pools at 1.6m ACD. Fucus serratus was abundant. Dumontia, Gigartina, Laurencia(2 spp), Laminaria digitata and Osteichthyes indet were frequent. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Middle shore broken bedrock with many rock ridges and small rockpools. Dominated by Porphyra with extensive cover, and Enteromorpha. A few fucoids (Fucus vesiculosus and F. serratus) in flatter areas, and patches of flat algal turf in damper areas and towards the lower middle shore. Barnacles were frequent (Chthamalus montagui, C. stellatus, Elminius modestus), with Semibalanus balanoides and Patella vulgata super abundant. Monodonta lineata, Actinia equina, Littorina menae and Hyale nilssoni were all frequent, and Gibbula umbilicalis occasional. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Lower shore. Broken bedrock with heavy algal cover of Fucus serratus with large amounts Laurencia pinnatifida, Gigartina stellata, Palmaria palmata and encrusting coralline algae. Laminaria digitata was abundant below. Balanus perforatus was common, with a variety of other animals occasional or frequent. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Lower shore boulders from ferry jetty to bedrock. All boulders were dominated by Fucus serratus, but cobbles and pebbles were chiefly bare with occasional cobbles covered in Enteromorpha sp and Ulva sp. Gigartina stellata was common on sides and under fucoids. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Lower shore bedrock and concrete slip. Thin area was Fucus serratus dominated but with a greater variety and abundance of red algae than on the boulders. Gelidium pusillum, Laurencia pinnatifida and encrusting Rhodophyta were most common here. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.
Upper mid shore bedrock slope dominated by Ascophyllum with scattered barnacles and Patella below. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Mid shore steeply sloping broken bedrock including vertical surfaces dominated by Ascophyllum and Fucus serratus with some large patches of rock dominated by barnacles and Patella. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.
Lower mid shore broken rock dominated by Fucus serratus, heavily epiphyted with Alcyonidium gelatinosum. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.
Lower shore broken bedrock dominated by Fucus serratus with common Balanus crenatus on rock. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Midshore bedrock. Dominated on steeply sloping and west facing bedrock planes by barnacles and limpets and on flats and sheltered slopes by Ascophyllum nodosum and fucoids chiefly Fucus serratus but some F. vesiculosus patches higher. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.
Lower mid shore and lower shore bedrock, dominated by Fucus serratus. A few areas limpet/barnacle dominated. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.
Lower shore dominated by Fucus serratus, and red and green algae. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Lower middle shore at 2m ACD as gently sloping bedrock. Heavy cover of Ascophyllum, with Fucus serratus occasional. Gelidium pusillum common, and a small variety of red and green algae frequent or occasional. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Middle shore. Ascophyllum covering a wide zone from 2 to 4m with dense cover. A few patches not covered with Ascophyllum were dominated by limpets and barnacles, mainly Elminius. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Lower shore bedrock. Similar to habitat 4, but with more frequent red algae including common Ceramium rubrum and lesser amounts of Antithamnion, Hypoglossum, Palmaria, Bryopsis plumosa and others. Large masses of Hymeniacidon near low water with abundant Balanus crenatus. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Wide midshore band dominated by Ascophyllum on broken bedrock with many steep and overhanging surfaces. Fucus serratus and F. vesiculosus occasional. Lower shore bedrock, including overhangs. Sand, pebble and cobble plain at 4 to 6m BCD. Here Cerianthus, Asterias, Gobius, Cancer, Pagurus and pink encrusting algae were all common. Algae as substratum, with frequent Palmaria and Porphyra and common Phycodrys. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Sand plain with cobbles at 4m BCD. Ceramium, Cryptopleura, Desmarestia, Laminaria saccharina and Ulva were all frequent. Lanice and Terebellids were abundant. Drift dive over sediment surface at 7m BCD. Rippled coarse sand sediment with a significant shell gravel content. The sediment showed many signs of burrowing activity with a prominent presence of Ensis ensis (siphons visible, also empty shells on surface), Bivalve siphons indet, Lanice conchylega (C), Terebellidae indet (large Lanice- like), Echinocardium cordatum (some empty casts on the surface). Occasional Cerianthus lloydii. Also Sagartiogeton undatus and Urticina felina were found as solitary individuals. Large numbers of drogonets Callionymus lyra were seen together with Gobidae indet (various sand gobies). Also large numbers of Asterias rubens and Carcinus maenas were seen on the surface. The surface was strewn with various sized mobile cobbles the larger ones being covered by Laminaria saccharina with attached hydroid and bryozoan growth. Smaller stones were largely colonised by Pomatoceros. Several fish species were noteably present especially Plaice and Pollack. Pleuronectes platessa was in the water column. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Algae as substrate. Obelia geniculata and Membranipora membranecea were abundant, Porphyra sp. and Cryptopleura ramosa were recorded as frequent. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

location_name: NE of Royal Fowey Yacht Club (Fowey)
sample_comment: Sediment shore Lower Midshore 2m ACD. Coarse sand gravel (1 to 2mm). Clean well sorted mobile high energy environment. No visible macrofaunal content. (0.1m2/0.01m2 sediment cores taken). This habitat represents the 0.1m2 data. Abundant Scolelepis squamata, Ophelia rathkei and oligochaetes were found in the core sample(entered as box quadrat). NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Lower middle shore bedrock. Dominated with Ascophyllum as middle shore, but with Mastocarpus stellatus common beneath. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Sediment spot sample (1.8m ACD LMS). Cores 0.1m2/1mm and 0.01m2/0.5mm. Sediment was quite stable sandy mud with a shallow sink becoming firm at a few cm's depth although dark no black anoxic layer was found within the surface layers down to 15cm depth. Large quantities of detritus from terrestrial origin were retained in the sieves. The smooth surface was scantily covered with whole Mya shells and unattached Enteromorpha. Also large numbers of springtails were seen feeding on the surface layers. Some digging around the site revealed the presence of live Mya arenaria in the sediments whereas digging in the more coarse sediment areas downstream revealed no live specimens possibly indicating the less stable and coarse nature of the sediment being less habitable. There was a large number of Arenicola casts visible on the surface and some specimens were retained in the sieved cores. Also Hediste diversicolor was common together with smaller polycheate infauna. No infaunal data available. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Thick silty mud with black anoxic layer and some small encrusted stones at 8 m bcd. The black anoxic layer was at a very shallow depth and the sediment was dominated by polychaetes. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Large cave ca. 20m deep, 5m high, and 1 to 2m wide with base at mid shore level. Vertical and steeply sloping walls with water seepage on south side. Patchy cover on walls, away from water seepage, including Catenella caespitosa, Audouinella sp., Enteromorpha sp. and filamentous green algae, and Lomentaria articulata. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

location_name: NE of Royal Fowey Yacht Club (Fowey)
sample_comment: Underside of slipway to yacht club, 1 to 2m ACD. The upper levels were dominated by Elminius modestus with strewn Enteromorpha and Ulva. Lower levels had strewn Fucus seratus with ectocarpoids abundant, and a macrofaunal presence of Balanus perforatus dominant with occasional E. modestus. Attached to the algae were abundant Dynamena pumila and Alcyonidium gelatinosum. Attached to the rock were abundant Balanus perforatus, littorinids and spirorbids. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.
Midshore. Barnacle/limpet dominated. No plants were recorded here. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.
Midshore. Barnacle/limpet dominated. No plants were recorded here. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Mid shore steeply sloping broken bedrock including vertical surfaces dominated by Ascophyllum and Fucus serratus with some large patches of rock dominated by barnacles and Patella. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Upper shore bedrock. Dominated in turn by yellow lichens, (Xanthoria parietina, Caloplaca thallicola, Caloplaca maritima) then Verrucaria maura, then Pelvetia canaliculata and finally Fucus spiralis. Under Pelvetia and fucoids in shade were Audouinella sp and Catenella caespitosa and in less shade were encrusting dark red Rhodophyta indet. and less commonly Verrucaria mucosa. In pools under Fucus spiralis were Laomedea flexuosa and Clava multicornis. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Midshore bedrock. Dominated on steeply sloping and west facing bedrock planes by barnacles and limpets and on flats and sheltered slopes by Ascophyllum nodosum and fucoids chiefly Fucus serratus but some F. vesiculosus patches higher. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Upper shore Pelvetia and Fucus spiralis on bedrock, steep in places but much flatter than most of the areas surveyed. Heavy fucoid cover and dense Catenella beneath Pelvetia. Occasional Bostrychia. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Upper shore short zones of Pelvetia and Fucus spiralis from 4 to 4.8m, with Enteromorpha, Bostrychia and Catenella both common and Hildenbrandia abundant. Anurida were extremely abundant; other animals included frequent Littorina saxitilis and amphipods. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Lower midshore overhangs (animals only). Bowerbankia and Cryptosula were abundant, Semibalanus and Patella were frequent. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Upper shore short bands of Pelvetia and Fucus spiralis on steep and broken bedrock, with Bostrychia and Catenella common and large cover of Hildenbrandia on rock surfaces. Amphipods were abundant at this level, and Littorina saxatilis frequent. Occasional Elminius. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Upper shore bedrock overhung by oakwood with Woodrush, Honeysuckle, Holly and ferns etc. Rock surfaces with green and grey lichens, no yellow lichens present in the shadiest places, being replaced by green powdery alga/lichen, and Enteromorpha. Below this dense Pelvetia/Bostrychia and Fucus spiralis/ Catenella bands, with Audouinella bands locally abundant in patches on bedrock and stones. Anurida, Petrobius, red mites and Littorina saxitilis were recorded. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Upper shore including splash zone. Lichens on steep bedrock, the usual upper shore Pelvetia/Bostrychia and F. spiralis/Catenella on flatter rock and more extensive bands. Enteromorpha was also abundant at this level, as were amphipods. Red mites were frequent, as well as Littorina saxitilis and L. littorea. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Middle shore under stones and overhangs. Carcinus maenus was abundant, Elminius modestus and Littorina saxitilis were frequent, whilst Enteromorpha was occasional.(Sub-habitat of 260.027.003) NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Upper shore under stones. Carcinuis maenus was frequent, Littorina saxitilis was occasional and Elminius modestus was rare.(Sub-habitat of 260.027.001) NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Upper shore on steepish broken bedrock with fairly well developed licens (shaded but not excessively so) and Pelvetia, Bostrychia and F. spiralis/Catenella bands. Hildenbrandia was common. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Upper shore under stones and boulders. Few species recorded but amphipods were super abundant, Anurida maritima was frequent and Littorina saxitilis was common. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Soft mud bank on north side with very large numbers of Nereis and Corophium volutator and sparse Scrubicularia plana. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Two cores taken here at 2.5m ACD; 0.1m2/1mm mesh and 0.01m2/0.5mm mesh. Anoxic silty mud. Black layer at shallow depth (around 5cm). Small numbers of whole shells cover the smooth surface (largely Scrobicularia plana). The sediment is glutinous with deep sink. Large numbers of Arenicola casts were visible and bait digging activities nearby indicated the presence of large indigenous populations. Macrofaunal content of cores was polycheate dominated, - mainly Hediste diversicolor which was superabundant. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Coarse sandy mud with gravel, strongly rippled surface with very shallow sink. Surface cover consisted of some whole shells mainly Scrobicularia and some Enteromorpha attached to small pebbles and stones on the surface. (Empty shells of Mya arenaria and Cerastoderma edule were also present). A few Arenicola casts were visible. Macrofaunal content was mainly low numbers of Hediste diversicolor and occasional Cerastoderma edule together with other smaller worms. A 0.5m2 dig revealed the presence of two live Scrobicularia. Two cores 0.1m2/1mm mesh, and 0.01m2/0.5mm mesh were taken at 1m ACD. No infaunal data available. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Increasingly coarse sediment sand bank, surface layers more stoney than habitat 2, and gravel content higher in general. Again large numbers of whole shells covering the surface. Scrobicularia plana, Cerastoderma edule and Hediste diversicolor were found within the 0.5m2 area dug. No live Mya were found in the area although they do occur here.No infaunal data available. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Mudflats ca. 150m downstream from Penroll Bridge. Colonised by Hediste diversicolor which was abundant, and by Scrobicularia which was frequent. Corophium was searched for but not found. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Mid shore and upper mid shore at 3 to 5m ACD. Steeply sloping slate broken in places. Dominated by limpets overgrown by Porphyra at the time of survney and with Fucus vesiculosus evesiculosus present. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Middle shore broken bedrock with many rock ridges and small rockpools. Dominated by Porphyra with extensive cover, and Enteromorpha. A few fucoids (Fucus vesiculosus and F. serratus) in flatter areas, and patches of flat algal turf in damper areas and towards the lower middle shore. Barnacles were frequent (Chthamalus montagui, C. stellatus, Elminius modestus), with Semibalanus balanoides and Patella vulgata super abundant. Monodonta lineata, Actinia equina, Littorina menae and Hyale nilssoni were all frequent, and Gibbula umbilicalis occasional. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Midshore bedrock. Dominated on steeply sloping and west facing bedrock planes by barnacles and limpets and on flats and sheltered slopes by Ascophyllum nodosum and fucoids chiefly Fucus serratus but some F. vesiculosus patches higher. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Splash zone on broken bedrock. Zone very short, c.1 to 1.5m vertical extent. Fairly well developed lichen zonation, usual species. Very large Ramelina. In shade the lichens (except for Verrucaria) largely replaced by Enteromorpha and mosses. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Upper shore Pelvetia and Fucus spiralis on bedrock, steep in places but much flatter than most of the areas surveyed. Heavy fucoid cover and dense Catenella beneath Pelvetia. Occasional Bostrychia. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Middle shore. Ascophyllum covering a wide zone from 2 to 4m with dense cover. A few patches not covered with Ascophyllum were dominated by limpets and barnacles, mainly Elminius. Frequent Fucus serratus and occasional F. vesiculosus were also present in the fucoid cover, with green algae frequent. Animals included abundant Littorina mariae, Dynamena pumila, Laomedea flexuosa and frequent Clava multicornis. Bowerbankia imbricata was common, with a small variety of other species. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Wide midshore band dominated by Ascophyllum on broken bedrock with many steep and overhanging surfaces. Fucus serratus and F. vesiculosus occasional. F. vesiculosus was the only fucoid on a large metal mooring loop. A small variety of red and green algae were recorded. Dynamena pumila and Laomedea flexuosa were super abundant on large brown algae. Semibalanus balanoides were common and Elminius modestus frequent; Patella vulgata and Littorina mariae common. Abundant amphipods and Carcinus maenus were recorded, and Clava multicornis was frequent. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Lower middle shore, broken bedrock, dominated by Ascophyllum but also with Fucus vesiculosus and F. serratus. Gigartina stellata and Enteromorpha were common. Audouinella, Callithamnion, encrusting red algae and Cladophora rupestris were all frequent. Dynamena and Laomedea were both common on algae. Mytilus edulis, Littorina menae, Balanus crenatus and Bowerbankia imbricata all frequent, and one patch c. 1m2 of vertical bedrock had c. 200 limpets, and large barnacle cover. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Middle shore broken bedrock dominated by Ascophyllum with much epiphytic Polysiphonia lanosa and occasional Fucus vesiculosus. Gelidium pusillum was abundant and Mastocarpus stellatus was frequent under the Ascophyllum. Elminius was common and Balanus crenatus and Semibalanus balanoides were both frequent. Abundant amphipods and Littorina littorea were recorded, and frequent Carcinus. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Lower middle shore bedrock, with Fucus vesiculosus and Fucus ceranoides were common. Enteromorpha was occasional as well as blue green algae. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Middle shore broken bedrock with heavy cover of Ascophyllum. A few Fucus vesiculosus at upper limits of Ascophyllum. Polysiphonia linosa was more abundant on the lowest Ascophyllum and absent at the top. Audouinella, Gelidium and Hildenbrandia were frequent. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Lower middle shore bedrock. Dominated with Ascophyllum as middle shore, but with Mastocarpus stellatus common beneath. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Lower mid shore and mid shore, on and under stones and boulders. Fucus vesiculosus abundant on muddy shale, with F. ceranoides at low water. Elahista was frequent on Fucus vesiculosus. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Very coarse sediment with broken slates and assorted stones, small cobbles and pebbles at 10.2 m bcd. The sediment contained many polychaetes with a very large count of Apseudes latreillii. Encrusting growth was found on the slates accounting for the presence of Pomatoceros lamarckii, Verruca stroemia, Dynamena pumila and dwarf Metridium senile. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Thick silty mud with black anoxic layer and some small encrusted stones at 8 m bcd. The black anoxic layer was at a very shallow depth and the sediment was dominated by polychaetes. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Broken bedrock at c.2m BCD. Overhangs, small caves and potholes - more silty than the main SE facing habitat and with some different species. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Sand plain with cobbles at 4m BCD. Ceramium, Cryptopleura, Desmarestia, Laminaria saccharina and Ulva were all frequent. Lanice and Terebellids were abundant. Hydractinia, Pomatoceros, Carcinus, Ensis and Callionymus were recorded as frequent. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Drift dive over sediment surface at 7m BCD. Rippled coarse sand sediment with a significant shell gravel content. The sediment showed many signs of burrowing activity with a prominent presence of Ensis ensis (siphons visible, also empty shells on surface), Bivalve siphons indet, Lanice conchylega (C), Terebellidae indet (large Lanice- like), Echinocardium cordatum (some empty casts on the surface). Occasional Cerianthus lloydii. Also Sagartiogeton undatus and Urticina felina were found as solitary individuals. Large numbers of drogonets Callionymus lyra were seen together with Gobidae indet (various sand gobies). Also large numbers of Asterias rubens and Carcinus maenas were seen on the surface. The surface was strewn with various sized mobile cobbles the larger ones being covered by Laminaria saccharina with attached hydroid and bryozoan growth. Smaller stones were largely colonised by Pomatoceros. Several fish species were noteably present especially Plaice and Pollack. Pleuronectes platessa was in the water column. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.
Epiphytes on Zostera included abundant small Lacuna vincta, much Obelia geniculata and a few algal species. Leaves were generally clean except for Obelia.

Algae as substrate. Anemonia viridis, Alcyonidium gelatinosum, Electra pilosa, Ceramium rubrum and Ulva sp. were all frequent. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.
Overhangs on the lower shore with a wide variety of ospecies present including large areas of Umbonula littoralis and common Grantia compressa.

Upward facing bedrock in the kelp forest at 1.5m BCD. Smooth rock slope at area surveyed but broken slate elsewhere. Dominated by encrusting brown algae 40% of which was overgrown by Balanus crenatus and about 5% by encrusting bryozoa. Few foliose algae but dense Laminaria hyperborea overall. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Vertical and overhanging bedrock including gullies at 1.5 to 3.5m BCD. Dominated by orange encrusting bryozoa with ascidians common and a few erect bryozoa and hydrozoa. Algae were common in places - particulary Corallina officinalis, Delesseria sanguinea and Dilsea carnosa. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Under boulders in pools at ca. 1.6m ACD. Gibbula cineraria was super abundant and Cirripedia indet, and Patella were abundant. Fucus sp, Actinia, Porcellana, Umbonula and Botrylloides were all frequent. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Kelp holdfasts at 9m BCD, in Upper Infralittoral. Scypha ciliata was frequent. Diplosoma listerianum, Lissoclinum perforatum and Polycarpa rustica were common. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Lower shore dominated by Fucus serratus, and red and green algae. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Lower middle shore at 2m ACD as gently sloping bedrock. Heavy cover of Ascophyllum, with Fucus serratus occasional. Gelidium pusillum common, and a small variety of red and green algae frequent or occasional. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Anurida were extremely abundant; other animals included frequent Littorina saxitilis and amphipods. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Wide midshore band dominated by Ascophyllum on broken bedrock with many steep and overhanging surfaces. Fucus serratus and F. vesiculosus occasional. F. vesiculosus was the only fucoid on a large metal mooring loop. A small variety of red and green algae were recorded. Dynamena pumila and Laomedea flexuosa were super abundant on large brown algae. Semibalanus balanoides were common and Elminius modestus frequent; Patella vulgata and Littorina mariae common. Abundant amphipods and Carcinus maenus were recorded, and Clava multicornis was frequent. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Lower midshore on broken bedrock with Fucus serratus dominant, and a small variety of small red algae. Ascophyllum was still frequent at this level, and green algae were common. Dynamena and Laomedea were both abundant on fucoids, and Clava frequent. Amphipods were abundant under fucoids, and Carcinus and Pomatoceros common. Littorina mariae was frequent and Littorina littorea was rare. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Lower shore bedrock, including overhangs. Large cover of Fucus serratus, with Ceramium rubrum becoming predominent at low water level (0.7m) and lesser abundance of a variety of small red and green algae. Dynamena, Laomedea and Alcyonidium were again abundant on fucoids. Balanus crenatus was common and Elminius frequent, while Pomatoceros was common. Abundant amphipods and frequent Carcinus were present under fucoids. Bowerbankia imbricata was common and Hymeniacidon frequent. Occasional Asterias were present. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Middle shore and lower middle shore crevices had Hymeniacidon, Carcinus and a few Actinia equina, Littorina littorea, Alcyonium mytili, Cryptosula pallasiana and Bowerbankia imbricata. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Under boulders on the lower middle shore. Carcinus and Terebellids were common, Elminius frequent. One Anguilla (juvenile) was recorded, and Littorina liittorea was occasional. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Middle shore broken bedrock dominated by Ascophyllum with much epiphytic Polysiphonia lanosa and occasional Fucus vesiculosus. Gelidium pusillum was abundant and Mastocarpus stellatus was frequent under the Ascophyllum. Elminius was common and Balanus crenatus and Semibalanus balanoides were both frequent. Abundant amphipods and Littorina littorea were recorded, and frequent Carcinus. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Middle shore bedrock, dominated by Ascophyllum. Much soft mud on ledges. Polysiphonia lanosa and Audouinella common; Cladophora rupestris and Gelidium pusillum were frequent. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Middle shore under stones and overhangs. Carcinus maenus was abundant, Elminius modestus and Littorina saxitilis were frequent, whilst Enteromorpha was occasional.(Sub-habitat of 260.027.003) NOTE Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Lower middle shore bedrock, with Fucus vesiculosus and Fucus ceranoides were common. Enteromorpha was occasional as well as blue green algae. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Lower middle shore under stones. Carcinus maenus was abundant, Mytilus edulis was frequent.(Sub-habitat of 260.027.005) NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Upper shore under stones. Carcinuis maenus was frequent, Littorina saxitilis was occasional and Elminius modestus was rare.(Sub-habitat of 260.027.001) NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Upper shore on steepish broken bedrock with fairly well developed lichens (shaded but not excessively so) and Pelvetia, Bostrychia and F. spiralis/Catenella bands. Hildenbrandia was common. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Upper shore under stones and boulders. Few species recorded but amphipods were super abundant, Anurida maritima was frequent and Littorina saxitilis was common. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Middle shore broken bedrock with heavy cover of Ascophyllum. A few Fucus vesiculosus at upper limits of Ascophyllum. Middle shore under stones. Species recorded were amphipods (EX), Carcinus maenus (SA), Littorina littorea (F) and Mytilus edulis (C). NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Lower middle shore bedrock. Dominated with Ascophyllum as middle shore, but with Mastocarpus stellatus common beneath. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Lower mid shore and mid shore, on and under stones and boulders. Fucus vesiculosus abundant on muddy shale, with F. ceranoides at low water. Elachista was frequent on Fucus vesiculosus. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Slope of mud and slate boulders on south shore below green. Fringed at the top (about +1.5 above stream bed) with filamentous green algae and boulders dominated by fucoids. Many amphipods and Sphaeromatidae under boulders. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Lower part of stone wall from stream bed to 1.5m dominated by Fucus ceranoides with frequent Elminius in interstices and a slime of diatoms and some ?Catenella. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Plain of quartz and slate cobbles in mud 5 to 6m. Level seabed with common Metridium senile and frequent Sagartiogeton. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Mud slope and hummocks with scattered quartz pebbles. Largely bare except for occasional Sagartiogeton undatus and mobile species. Reason for hummocked appearance not clear but could be dredging (hummocks run N - S). NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Quartz pebbles and cobbles on mud. A steep slope 2m high with area at the top covered by pebbles and cobbles swarming with small Pagurus. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Broken bedrock at +0.3m with heavy cover of foliose algae and Laminaria saccharina just below. Mainly Ceramium rubrum with a good variety of other algae. Animals sparse, except for encrusting bryozoans on algae and some sponges. Tiny prosobranchs Lacuna vincta collected with algae and probably abundant. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substrtaum 1-3 scoring system.

Offshore on mud which has obviously been dredged. Mud as habitat 4, ie soft on surface, compacted, black and hard beneath. Bottom with crators of mud 'cliffs', very uneven. Abundant Crangon on surface, occasional Carcinus, otherwise no sign of life apart from numerous small tracks in the soft surface mud. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Dense algae on firm muddy sand. Abundant Laminaria saccharina and common Ceramium rubrum and Ulva and a variety of others frequent. Few animals were recorded, mainly encrusting bryozoans on algal fronds. Caprellids, Mysids were frequent. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Patchy Zostera, dense in places, with much Laminaria saccharina mixed in particulary in shallow water. Much foliose algae as drift around Zostera bases, and also attached. Chorda and Gracilaria were frequent and Chondria rare. Epiphytes on Zostera are noted under habitat 4. Cereus was frequent in sediment, and Hinia and Sand gobies frequent on the surface, otherwise few animals were seen. Some holes in the sediment were observed. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Mud plain and slope. On mud were occasional Plaice, Flounders and Sand gobies but most abundant were hermit crabs (Paguridae indet), Nassarius reticulatus, Crangon crangon and Carcinus maenas. There were one or two Cerianthus lloydii. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

location_name: No. 3 Quay, S of Upper Carn Point (Fowey)
sample_comment: Level seabed of stones on mud at ca 6m. Virtually bare of epibiota except scattered Metridium senile and included here only for the presence of one Calliactis parasitica on Pagurus cuanensis. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Sand plain with cobbles at 4m BCD. Ceramium, Cryptopleura, Desmarestia, Laminaria saccharina and Ulva were all frequent. Lanice and Terebellids were abundant. Hydractinia, Pomatoceros, Carcinus, Ensis and Callionymus were recorded as frequent. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Drift dive over sediment surface at 7m BCD. Rippled coarse sand sediment with a significant shell gravel content. The sediment showed many signs of burrowing activity with a prominent presence of Ensis ensis (siphons visible, also empty shells on surface), Bivalve siphons indet, Lanice conchylega (C), Terebellidae indet (large Lanice- like), Echinocardium cordatum (some empty casts on the surface). Occasional Cerianthus lloydii. Also Sagartiogeton undatus and Urticina felina were found as solitary individuals. Large numbers of drogonets Callionymus lyra were seen together with Gobidae indet (various sand gobies). Also large numbers of Asterias rubens and Carcinus maenas were seen on the surface. The surface was strewn with various sized mobile cobbles the larger ones being covered by Laminaria saccharina with attached hydroid and bryozoan growth. Smaller stones were largely colonised by Pomatoceros. Several fish species were noteably present especially Plaice and Pollack. Pleuronectes platessa was in the water column. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Predominently muddy shell gravel with stones at 3.3m ACD. An area of 0.1m2 excavated to 20cm and sieved over 1mm mesh (coarse material discarded, 1 Carcinus maenus not preserved) 0.01m2 dug to 10cm and sieved over a 0.5mm mesh. The infauna consisted of superabundant Hediste diversicolor and abundant oligochaetes and Abra alba. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Clayey mud without any shale at 2.5m ACD. No obvious black layer. Very poorly colonised. 0.1m2 excavated to 20cm and sieved over a 1mm mesh (1 Carcinus maenus juvenile not preserved). NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Clayey mud without any shale at 2.5m ACD. No obvious black layer. Very poorly colonised. 0.1m2 excavated to 20cm and sieved over a 1mm mesh (1 Carcinus maenus juvenile not preserved). NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Under stones in stream near Penpoll Bridge. Here Anguilla and Elminius were frequent. Cymodoce and amphipods were common. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.
Stones and boulders on the lower shore at stream. Majority of species recorded were only noted as 'present'. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Mixture of coarse sand and gravel much the same as site D1. Worms again constituted the majority of the macrofauna with Pygospio elegans occuring in relatively high numbers when compared to the other fauna. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Very coarse sediment with broken slates and assorted stones, small cobbles and pebbles at 10.2 m bcd. The sediment contained many polychaetes with a very large count of Apseudes latreillii. Encrusting growth was found on the slates accounting for the presence of Pomatoecros lamarckii, Verruca stroemia, Dynamena pumila and dwarf Metridium senile. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Upward facing bedrock in the kelp forest at 1.5m BCD. Smooth rock slope at area surveyed but broken slate elsewhere. Dominated by encrusting brown algae 40% of which was overgrown by Balanus crenatus and about 5% by encrusting bryozoa. Few foliose algae but dense Laminaria hyperborea overall. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Slate bedrock with some small overhangs at 9m BCD. Bonnemaisonia asparagoides, Brongniartella byssoides, Dilsea carnosa, Plocamium cartilagineum, Polyneura hilliae, Radicilingua thysanorhizans, Rhodophyta indet, Schottera nicaeensis and Dictyota dichotoma were all frequent. Of the animals Hemimycale columella, Haliclona viscosa, Caryophyllia smithii, Marthasterias glacialis, Echinus esculentus and Labrus bergylta were likewise frequent in their abundance. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Kelp holdfasts at 9m BCD, in Upper Infralittoral. Scypha ciliata was frequent. Diplosoma listerianum, Lissoclinum perforatum and Polycarpa rustica were common. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Upper infralittoral broken bedrock at 0.9 to 1.4m BCD. Fairly dense foliose algae on silty horizontal ledges, steep and overhanging faces with some animals, mainly turfs. Scattered very large sponges (green pipes) Ciocalypta penicillus. Polyneura hilliae abundant, Laminaria hyperborea rare, and Laminaria saccharina common. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Lower infralittoral broken bedrock at 2.7 to 4.2m BCD. Thick sticky mud in hollows, and muddy rock surfaces. Fairly wide variety of scattered algae, including Stenogramme interrupta unusually on bedrock. Laminaria saccharina was rare. There was no L. hyperborea. Several characteristic silt tolerent species (Phyllophora crispa, P. pseudoceranoides, Cordylecladia erecta). Laminaria saccharina fronds loaded with Celleporella sp. Several fish - Goldsinnys and Scorpion fish. Many large sponges on ridges. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Infralittoral bedrock at 0 to 1.5m BCD. Broken rock platform dominated by filamentous algae (20 - 60%) and red foliose algae in upper part foliose more abundant 1 to 1.5m. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Upper circalittoral bedrock 1.5 to 3m BCD. Upward facing rock ledges dominated by tube dwelling amphipods and Maldanidae? with Suberites domuncula. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Sublittoral fringe ledges and overhangs at 0.5 to 0m BCD. Silty with a turf of ?Ceramium rubrum and scattered foliose red algae on upward facing surfaces and a small variety of animals, most conspicuously Clavellina lepadiformis and Leucosolenia botryoides. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Algae on Laminaria hyperborea stipe at 0.3 m BSL. Polyneura hilliae and Lomentaria articulata were recorded here.(Sub-habitat of 260.020.001) NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Dense algae on firm muddy sand. Abundant Laminaria saccharina and common Ceramium rubrum and Ulva and a variety of others frequent. Few animals were recorded, mainly encrusting bryozoans on algal fronds. Caprellids, Mysids were frequent. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Patchy Zostera, dense in places, with much Laminaria saccharina mixed in particulary in shallow water. Much foliose algae as drift around Zostera bases, and also attached. Chorda and Gracilaria were frequent and Chondria rare. Epiphytes on Zostera are noted under habitat 4. Cereus was frequent in sediment, and Hinia and Sand gobies frequent on the surface, otherwise few animals were seen. Some holes in the sediment were observed. Sublittoral fringe mooring blocks and chains. Luxuriant weed growth on older mooring blocks with Laminaria saccharina most evident, with Ulva sp. Ceramium rubrum, Cystoclonium purpureum and Desmarestia aculeata all large and frequent. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Steep very broken rock with rockmills, crevices, steep slopes and horizontal tops with abundant Laminaria hyperborea and foliose algae undercover in places, Corallina sp was dominant but overall was common. Delesseria, Callophyllis and Cryptopleura were common on the tops and Phyllophora and Polyneura were common on the steep sides of the rock. Encrusting orange bryozoans were abundant on steep faces especially in rockmills. There was heavy encrusting fauna on many algae. Colonial ascidians were common, Botrylloides and Morchellium were most common and of the sponges Hymeniacidon was frequent. Alcyonium and Caryophillia were occasional. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Here the Laminaria hyperborea was frequent whilst Laminaria saccharina was common. Encrusting dark red algae were abundant and pink encrusting algae were common, Ahnfeltia, Phyllophora, Polyneura, Callophyllis and Phyllophora were frequent. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Mid shore and upper mid shore at 3 to 5m ACD. Steeply sloping slate broken in places. Dominated by limpets overgrown by Porphyra at the time of survey and with Fucus vesiculosus evesiculosus present. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.
Overhangs on the lower shore kwith a wide variety of species present including large areas of Umbonula littoralis and common Grantia compressa.

Pools at ca. 4.5m ACD (partly in deep shade). Cladophora sp., Corallina officinalis and rhodophyta indet were all abundant. Filamentous brown algae and Grantia compressa were both frequent. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Broken bedrock at c.2m BCD. Overhangs, small caves and potholes - more silty than the main SE facing habitat and with some different species. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Overhangs and vertical rock at 0.8 to 1.8m ACD. Dominated by red algal turf, predominently Lomentaria articulata, Ceramium flabelligerum and Gigartina stellata. Animals included common encrusting bryozoans (Flustrellidra hispida, Electra pilosa), a few sponges (frequent Halichondria panicea and occasional Hymeniacidon perleve) Frequent Pomatoceros sp. and Dynamena pumila. One Nucella lapillus, the only specimen found on the shore. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Middle shore broken bedrock with many rock ridges and small rockpools. Dominated by Porphyra with extensive cover, and Enteromorpha. A few fucoids (Fucus vesiculosus and F. serratus) in flatter areas, and patches of flat algal turf in damper areas and towards the lower middle shore. Barnacles were frequent (Chthamalus montagui, C. stellatus, Elminius modestus), with Semibalanus balanoides and Patella vulgata super abundant. Monodonta lineata, Actinia equina, Littorina menae and Hyale nilssoni were all frequent, and Gibbula umbilicalis occasional. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Lower shore. Broken bedrock with heavy algal cover of Fucus serratus with large amounts Laurencia pinnatifida, Gigartina stellata, Palmaria palmata and encrusting coralline algae. Laminaria digitata was abundant below. Balanus perforatus was common, with a variety of other animals occasional or frequent. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Midshore overhanging rock near cave entrance with scattered barnacles and Actinia equina and abundant Plumularia elegans. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Lower shore overhang near cave entrance. Dominated by Cryptosula pallasiana with Grantia compressa and Halichondria panicea. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Mid shore steeply sloping broken bedrock including vertical surfaces dominated by Ascophyllum and Fucus serratus with some large patches of rock dominated by barnacles and Patella. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Lower shore dominated by Fucus serratus, and red and green algae. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Middle shore dominated by Alcyonium. Bedrock with gentle slope. Gelidium pusillum forming a turf beneath, with few other species. Fucus vesiculosus was occasional. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Lower middle shore at 2m ACD as gently sloping bedrock. Heavy cover of Ascophyllum, with Fucus serratus occasional. Gelidium pusillum common, and a small variety of red and green algae frequent or occasional. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Lower shore bedrock. Similar to habitat 4, but with more frequent red algae including common Ceramium rubrum and lesser amounts of Antithamnion, Hypoglossum, Palmaria, Bryopsis plumosa and others. Large masses of Hymeniacidon near low water with abundant Balanus crenatus. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Lower shore bedrock, including overhangs. Large cover of Fucus serratus, with Ceramium rubrum becoming predominent at low water level (0.7m) and lesser abundance of a variety of small red and green algae Dynamena, Laomedea and Alcyonidium were again abundant on fucoids. Balanus crenatus was common and Elminius frequent, while Pomatoceros was common. Abundant amphipods and frequent Carcinus were present under fucoids. Bowerbankia imbricata was common and Hymeniacidon frequent. Occasional Asterias were present. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Middle shore and lower middle shore crevices had Hymeniacidon, Carcinus and a few Actinia equina, Littorina littorea, Alcyonium mytili, Cryptosula pallasiana and Bowerbankia imbricata. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Middle shore under overhangs. Barnacles, Elminius and Balanus crenatus were frequent, as were Littorina obtusata/mariae and encrusting bryozoans. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Verical rockwall in sublittoral fringe at 0 to 0.5m BCD. Dominated by Corallina which was almost 100% cover. Chondrus was frequent. Phorbas fictitius? and Bugula plumosa were also frequent. Haliclystus was abundant. NOTE: Substratum  %data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Bedrock overhangs in sublittoral fringe at 0 to 0.5m BCD. Here an interesting encrusting faunal growth was noted including Halichondria panicea, Myxilla incrustans, Scypha ciliata, Corynactis viridis, dwarf Metridium senile and Botrylloides leachi. Upper circalittoral broken bedrock, not so many overhangs as habitats 2 and 3. A few algae (Polyneura laciniata, Myriogramme heterocarpum) at 5.6m BCD; many more at 4.6m BCD, but still typical upper circalittoral species mainly. Animals turfs generally dominant - hydroids, bryozoans and large sponges including Suberites. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Infralittoral bedrock at 0 to 1.5m BCD. Broken rock platform dominated by filamentous algae (20 - 60%) and red foliose algae in upper part foliose more abundant 1 to 1.5m. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Upper circalittoral bedrock 1.5 to 3m BCD. Upward facing rock ledges dominated by tube dwelling amphipods and Maldanidae? with Suberites domuncula. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Vertical or overhanging bedrock 3 to 5m dominated by a turf of Bugula turbinata and Scrupocellaria with sponges and hydroids over rock covered by tubiculous amphipods. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Sublittoral fringe ledges and overhangs at 0.5 to 0m BCD. Broken rock with extensive vertical, overhanging and upward facing rock dominated by tubiculous amphipods, with scattered foliose algae on upward facing surfaces and by Bugula turbinata and various hydroids on vertical surfaces. Broken bedrock at +0.3m with heavy cover of foliose algae and Laminaria saccharina just below. Mainly Ceramium rubrum with a good variety of other algae. Steep very broken rock with rockmills, crevices, steep slopes and horizontal tops with abundant Laminaria hyperborea and foliose algae undercover in places, Corallina sp was dominant but overall was common. Delesseria, Callophyllis and Cryptopleura were common on the tops and Phyllophora and Polyneura were common on the steep sides of the rock. Encrusting orange bryozoans were abundant on steep faces especially in rockmills. There was heavy encrusting fauna on many algae. Colonial ascidians were common, Botrylloides and Morchellium were most common and of the sponges Hymeniacidon was frequent. Alcyonium and Caryophillia were occasional. Here the Laminaria hyperborea was frequent whilst Laminaria saccharina was common. Encrusting dark red algae were abundant and pink encrusting algae were common, Ahnfeltia, Phyllophora, Polyneura, Callophyllis and Phyllophora were frequent. Under boulders in pools at ca. 1.6m ACD. Gibbula cineraria was super abundant and Cirripedia indet, and Patella were abundant. Fucus sp, Actinia, Porcellana, Umbonula and Botrylloides were all frequent. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Patches of stones and pebbles in gravel at 9m BCD. Dilsea carnosa, Grateloupia filicina, Peyssonelia sp, Laminaria saccharina, Sphacelaria sp, Enteromorpha sp, and Audouinella sp were all frequent. Vertical and overhanging bedrock including gullies at 1.5 to 3.5m BCD. Dominated by orange encrusting bryozoa with ascidians common and a few erect bryozoa and hydrozoa. Algae were common in places - particulary Corallina officinalis, Delesseria sanguinea and Dilsea carnosa. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Overhang along concrete slip. This was overhung by Fucus serratus and the animal community was quite varied with sponges and ascidians, hydroids and bryozoans. Alcyonidium gelatinosum was abundant as were Pomatoceros sp. Balanus crenatus, and Dynamena pumila. Asterias rubens, Sertularia polyzonias, Halichondria panicea and Umbonula littoralis were frequent, and Leucosolenia botryoides, Botrylloides leachii, Clavelina lepadiformis, Morchellium argus and Sidnyum turbinatum were occasional. Plain of quartz and slate cobbles in mud 5 to 6m. Level seabed with common Metridium senile and frequent Sagartiogeton. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.
Mud with slate pebbles at 0 to 2m BCD. Most conspicuously colonised by Sagartiogeton undatus. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Mud slope and hummocks with scattered quartz pebbles. Largely bare except for occasional Sagartiogeton undatus and mobile species. Reason for hummocked appearance not clear but could be dredging (hummocks run N - S). NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Silt slope with small stones from 0.5 to 1.5m BSL. Few foliose algae and Laminaria saccharina attached to stones. Gracilaria was frequent, occasional Sagartiogeton. Frequent hermit crabs and occasional Crangon. Sand gobies were frequent on the slope with occasional Balanus crenatus. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Beyond the Zostera bed, a flat featureless sediment plain with few algae and visible animals. Mollusc siphons were observed once. Cereus was frequent, Sagartiogeton occasional and Cerianthus was rare. Several hermit crabs were seen, one with Hydractinia. Plaice were occasional and Sand gobies frequent and one dragonet was seen. A small Tonicella was found lying on the sediment. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Sulbittoral fringe mooring blocks and chains. Luxuriant weed growth on older mooring blocks with Laminaria saccharina most evident, with Ulva sp. Ceramium rubrum, Cystoclonium purpureum and Desmarestia aculeata all large and frequent. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Algae as substrate. Anemonia viridis, Alcyonidium gelatinosum, Electra pilosa, Ceramium rubrum and Ulva sp. were all frequent. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Drift dive over sediment surface at 7m BCD. Rippled coarse sand sediment with a significant shell gravel content. The sediment showed many signs of burrowing activity with a prominent presence of Ensis ensis (siphons visible, also empty shells on surface), Bivalve siphons indet, Lanice conchylega (C), Terebellidae indet (large Lanice- like), Echinocardium cordatum (some empty casts on the surface). Occasional Cerianthus lloydii. Also Sagartiogeton undatus and Urticina felina were found as solitary individuals. Large numbers of drogonets Callionymus lyra were seen together with Gobidae indet (various sand gobies). Also large numbers of Asterias rubens and Carcinus maenas were seen on the surface. The surface was strewn with various sized mobile cobbles the larger ones being covered by Laminaria saccharina with attached hydroid and bryozoan growth. Smaller stones were largely colonised by Pomatoceros. Several fish species were noteably present especially Plaice and Pollack. Pleuronectes platessa was in the water column. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Lower mid shore at 2 to 3m ACD. Broken slate dominated by Fucus serratus with Lomentaria articulata, Palmaria palmata and other algae. Fw barnacles present except for large numbers of Balanus perfoliata and animal species including common Dynamena pumila. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Lower shore steeply sloping slate at 0.7 to 1.5m ACD. NOTE: Substratum % data are convertde from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Pools at ca.1.5m to ca.50m from point. Corallina officinalis and rhodophyta indet were abundant. Fucus serratus, Himanthalia elongata and Mastocarpus stellatus were all present. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Encrusting orange bryozoans were abundant on steep faces especially in rockmills. There was heavy encrusting fauna on many algae. Colonial ascidians were common, Botrylloides and Morchellium were most common and of the sponges Hymeniacidon was frequent. Alcyonium and Caryophillia were occasional. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Patchy Zostera, dense in places, with much Laminaria saccharina mixed in particulary in shallow water. Much foliose algae as drift around Zostera bases, and also attached. Upper shore bedrock. Dominated in turn by yellow lichens, (Xanthoria parietina, Caloplaca thallicola, Caloplaca maritima) then Verrucaria maura, then Pelvetia canaliculata and finally Fucus spiralis. Under Pelvetia and fucoids in shade were Audouinella sp and Catenella caespitosa and in less shade were encrusting dark red Rhodophyta indet. and less commonly Verrucaria mucosa. In pools under Fucus spiralis were Laomedea flexuosa and Clava multicornis. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Splash zone on broken bedrock. Zone very short, c.1 to 1.5m vertical extent. Fairly well developed lichen zonation, usual species. Very large Ramelina. In shade the lichens (except for Verrucaria) largely replaced by Enteromorpha and mosses. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Splash zone above 4.8m ACD. Short zone ca 1m vertically with the usual zonation on steep and vertical bedrock. A particulary wide variety of flowering plants encroaching from the cliff above.
